name: Student Engagement
description: Student Engagement includes the following departments: Center for Student Engagement, Fraternity and Sorority Life, Fitness & Recreational Sports, and ASUN's Department of Clubs & Orgs. We provide support and resources to all recognized student organizations on campus.
summary: Student Engagement is a hub for information related to student clubs at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: New Student Initiatives
description: NSI runs new student orientation, welcome events like Opening Ceremony and Opening Weekend, and events and programs throughout the academic year. Our mission is to help first-year and new transfer students become engaged in University life and experience a great start to their college experience.
summary: We help new students at Nevada succeed academically, socially and emotionally in their first year.

name: Student Persistence Research Office
description:
Provides support for retention research and learning outcomes assessment for the Division of Student Services. 

 

Use the "Project Planning" form below to request help (you must be signed into Pack Life to view this form)

Or

See the documents below for standardized assessment questions and a checklist for planning programs.
summary: Retention research and assessment office for the Division of Student Services.

name: The Multicultural Center
description: The Multicultural Center is a critical hub of cultural, LGBTQIA, gender-focused services, programs, and social justice initiatives that support student success and increase graduation rates of students at the University of Nevada, Reno. The Center empowers students to be innovative colleagues in creating a better-integrated community at the University and beyond.
summary: A critical hub of cultural, LGBTQIA, gender-focused services, programs, and social justice initiatives that support student success and graduation.

name: Fitness & Recreational Sports
description: Fitness and Recreational Sports (FRS) is committed to the development of a healthy learning environment supportive of the internal quality of campus life, a quality of life that fosters personal growth and enhances creative, scholarly activity by providing quality fitness and recreational sport services that complement the University of Nevada, Reno's mission.
summary: A leader in providing quality fitness and recreational sport opportunities to the campus community that promote and encourage the pursuit of lifelong fitness.

name: Vice President of Student Services
summary: We exist to recruit, retain, and graduate students. The Division of Student Services remains committed to our students and our campus community through this pandemic.

name: Fraternity and Sorority Life
description: Our community is home to 25 organizations. These organizations are governed by student-led boards called councils. At the University of Nevada, Reno, we have three different councils that provide governance, accountability, educational opportunities, and structure for our fraternities and sororities: the Interfraternity Council (IFC), Multicultural Greek Council (MGC) and National Panhellenic Conference (NPC). To learn more about each council and the organizations they govern, please reference our governance page.
summary: Our community is home to 25 organizations. These organizations are governed by student-led boards called councils.

name: ASUN Executive Board
summary: The ASUN Executive Board is comprised of elected and appointed officials. Who represent the student body through our EMPOWER Nevada Initiatives.

name: ASUN Senate
description:
Since 1933, the Senate of the Associated Students has represented the students of the University of Nevada through a number of events, legislation and active community engagement.

The Senate meets every Wednesday at 5:30 p.m. during the fall and spring Semester.

The Senate meets every other Wednesday at 5:30 p.m. during the summer. 

Visit http://www.nevadaasun.com/meetings/ for meeting agendas
summary: The Senate is the Legislative Branch of ASUN. It is composed of the Speaker of the Senate and 25 elected Senators. Senators serve as legislators, writing and amending bills and resolutions that reflect needs and voices of students.

name: ASUN Judicial
description: The ASUN Judicial Council is composed of a Chief Justice and four Associate Justices. The council has the responsibility of maintaining high standards of conduct through the review of submitted cases.
summary: This branch makes sure that the Executive and Senate branches are upholding the legal documents.

name: ASUN Government Affairs
description: The ASUN Department of Government Affairs is dedicated to educating, empowering, and encouraging students to get involved in University, Local, State, National, and Global issues.
summary: The ASUN Department of Government Affairs lobbies for students on local, state, and national levels.

name: ASUN IDEA
description: The ASUN Department of Diversity and Inclusion is led by the Director of Diversity and Inclusion. The department aims to serve underrepresented student populations on campus and to make every student feel welcome at the University no matter their religion, race, socioeconomic status, sexual orientation/preference, etc. To achieve true inclusiveness, we seek social justice through policies, events, and initiatives geared towards underrepresented students and their needs.
summary: The ASUN Department of IDEA aims to serve underrepresented student populations on campus.

name: ASUN Programming
description: The primary mission of the ASUN Department of Event Programming is to inspire, unite, and provide educational opportunities to the University of Nevada students through Event Programming derived from Nevada Traditions, cultural diversity, large-scale entertainment, and athletics. The board will execute the laws, planning and management of all official department events of the Associated Students of the University of Nevada.
summary: The ASUN Department of Programming plans and implements all ASUN events.

name: ASUN Clubs and Organizations
description: The ASUN Department of Clubs and Organizations provides resources to more than 200 student clubs and organizations at the University of Nevada, Reno.
summary: The ASUN Department of Clubs and Organizations provides resources to more than 200 student clubs and organizations at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: Residential Life, Housing, and Food Services
description: The Residential Life, Housing, and Food Services department serves all on-campus residents, as well as anyone who dines on campus. Located in Juniper Hall (on the lake side) at the south end of campus. RLHFS is also home to the Residence Hall Association and National Residence Hall Honorary organizations that program and advocate for students living within the residence halls.
summary: The Residential Life, Housing, and Food Services department serves all on-campus residents, as well as anyone who dines on campus. Located in Juniper Hall (on the lake side) at the south end of campus.

name: Dean of Students Office
description: The Dean of Students Office delivers programs and support to foster a culture of empowerment, care, and accountability for all students.
summary: The Dean of Students Office delivers programs and support to foster a culture of empowerment, care, and accountability for all students.

name: Admissions and Records
summary: The Office of Admissions and Records provides a range of services, ranging from transcripts requests, verifying enrollment, verifying degrees awarded to our students and alumni, and residency requests.

name: Center for Student Engagement
description:
The Center for Student Engagement cultivates student success by providing an equitable and inclusive environment of nationally-recognized educational programs and services. We develop courageous and civically engaged students who use their voices to be global leaders. We challenge systems that prevent student’s full participation in university life.

Vision

We develop future leaders who will create an equitable and inclusive world.

Values

  • Equity and Inclusion
  • Learning
  • Service
  • Leadership
  • Community

The Center for Student Engagement provides advising to ASUN's appointed and elected officers, all clubs and organizations and its publications. Additionally, we provide learning opportunities for students to enhance their overall educational experience through their involvement with the programs and services funded by ASUN, the University and the community.

The Center for Student Engagement recognizes that its mission, in cooperation with the learning mission of the University, is accomplished through cultivating a personal standard of ethics and values while providing opportunities that enhance a sense of community between students, the campus, and the local community.
summary: The Center for Student Engagement works collaboratively to engage, educate and empower undergraduate students to take action in the best interest of the student body and the University community.

name: Student Health Center
summary: The Student Health Center provides high-quality and accessible health care and education to our diverse student community and promotes lifelong physical and emotional well-being. We are dedicated to providing a safe and welcoming environment for all.

name: University Studies Abroad Consortium
description:
USAC, a nonprofit organization since 1982, has provided students with life-changing opportunities to live and learn in countries around the globe. We have more than 50 affordable, authentic study abroad program in 26 countries, and strive to provide you with ample opportunities to become engaged, global citizens. 

Our headquarters are located right here on campus, but students from any university are welcome to participate on USAC programs. That means not only will you make friends and connections in your host country, but you can also engage and network with peers from all over the United States while you explore another country and culture together. And because we are your study abroad office, University of Nevada, Reno students like you have access to special resources to help you plan your study abroad experience.

Learn how to get started here.
summary: USAC offers the opportunity for students to live and learn in countries around the world through study abroad opportunities in over 50+ locations.

name: Undergraduate Research
description:
Being involved in research, scholarship and creative activity outside of the classroom not only offers a unique way to explore your interests, but will help prepare you for the highly competitive job market. Undergraduate students’ exposure to these opportunities is generally very limited. Having research experience will give you an edge in your career field or in your pursuit of a graduate degree—an option you may not have even considered before.

Doing research doesn’t only mean working in a lab…it can be anything from a photographic analysis of a changing landscape over time to studying salt-loving plants to improve crop production in marginal lands. Whether your interests are in the arts, sciences, journalism, education or another field, our office is here to help you expand your experience while at the University.
summary: Undergraduate Research provides funded research opportunities for undergraduates conducting research, scholarship, and creative activity. Free workshops, trainings, and symposia available.

name: Welcome Center, Office for Prospective Students
summary: Home to the University campus visit operation, we offer tours and visit programs to prospective students and their families.

name: Nevada Money Mentors
description: We want all students to feel solid about their financial choices. In 2016, we started Nevada Money Mentors with one goal: Empower you with financial knowledge so you make informed, confident decisions about money both while here at Nevada and after college.
summary: Nevada Money Mentors is a financial wellness program on campus that helps to empower students with financial knowledge so they can make confident decisions with their money.

name: Joe Crowley Student Union
description: Students feel welcomed and make themselves at home in the Joe Crowley Student Union. The Joe offers focused study spaces, well-appointed meeting rooms, stunning special event venues, and an abundance of dining options. This cultural anchor of our beautiful campus opened on November 16, 2007 and continues to be a student-centered building complementing the academic experience through cultural, educational, social and recreational programs. As the living room of campus, The Joe is committed to providing a safe, inclusive and comfortable space for all members of our diverse Wolf Pack community.
summary: The Joe offers everything from study spaces, dining options, event and meeting venues, and more. As the living room of campus, we are committed to providing a safe, inclusive and comfortable space for all members of our diverse Wolf Pack community.

name: Office of International Students and Scholars
summary: Our staff members have extensive knowledge and experience in the international education field and work with students, scholars, and community members to help enrich their study and research experiences at the University.

name: Disability Resource Center
description: The DRC is dedicated to providing a coordinated program of support services which are not furnished by other university offices or outside organizations. The DRC assists students in negotiating disability related barriers and strives to improve access and opportunity. This enables all levels of students with disabilities to become integrated into campus life, and become more successful undergraduate or graduate students while maximizing their independence. Our services are free of charge.
summary: The DRC assists students in negotiating disability related barriers and strives to improve access and opportunity. This enables all levels of students with disabilities to become integrated into campus life, maximize independence and be more successful.

name: Division of Student Services
summary: The Division of Student Services exists to recruit, retain, and graduate students.

name: Scheduling Services
description: The Scheduling Services office manages University-wide space allocation for academic instruction, non-academic space usage, space rental for external events and activities.
summary: The Scheduling Services office manages University-wide space allocation for academic instruction, non-academic space usage, space rental for external events and activities.

name: Women of Color Collective
description: The purpose of Women of Color Collective is to engage in community care by providing a safe, supportive, and welcoming space for Asian & Pacific Islander, Black, Latinx, Middle Eastern/North African, Native American, and other women-identifying people of color. All events are available to any students interested in our mission. This program is a collaboration between various departments at the University of Nevada, Reno.
summary: A safe, supportive, and welcoming space for Asian & Pacific Islander, Black, Latinx, Middle Eastern/North African, Native American, and other women-identifying people of color.

name: Nevada Career Studio
description:
Let us help you with your career development: 

  • Resumes, cover letters, personal statements
  • Mock interviews (in person and by video)
  • Job boards for opportunities on and off-campus
  • Search for jobs and internships
  • Figure out what to do with your major
  • Connect you to employers at our fairs and events
summary: The Nevada Career Studio’s mission is to connect students, educators, and employers to meet the workforce development needs of the state, nation and global community by building student career readiness from early engagement, graduation and beyond

name: Counseling Services
description:
Hello! We are Counseling Services! Click here to visit our website. 

We provide mental health resources to the students of the University of Nevada, Reno campus. Our services are included in the cost of tuition for most students that are enrolled in one or more credit - this means most students are able to use our services at no additional cost.

 

We offer Let's Talk drop-in consultations, group therapy, individual therapy, and we host a ton of events. Visit our website to learn more about Let's Talk and therapy services, and check out below to see our upcoming events!
summary: Counseling Services provides culturally responsive mental health services to the University of Nevada, Reno students regardless of their identities and/or immigration status.

name: DeLaMare Science and Engineering Library
description: The DeLaMare Science and Engineering Library is a technology-rich environment that provides growing user-centered services to the University. DeLaMare is home to an exploratory Makerspace, a bike lab, the Mary B. Ansari Map Library, and Data and GIS Depot. The Library offers popular GIS and Research Data Services, providing support for data management, analysis, statistics, and visualization. Poster and regular-size printing is available, as well as other lendable technology. Collections at the Library support the fields of chemistry, computer science, earth sciences, engineering, math, and physics.
summary: DLM Design, Learn, Make!

name: University of Nevada, Reno School of Medicine
description: For 50 years the University of Nevada, Reno School of Medicine has prepared physicians who are leading the way to better health for Nevada, the nation and the world. More than 1,800 M.D. graduates have received advanced education to provide the best in service to the fields of primary and specialty care, as well as medical research.
summary: Established in 1969, UNR Med is improving the health and well-being of all Nevadans and their communities through excellence in education, training, clinical care and research.

name: Veteran Services
description:
The Veteran Services Office provides a wide range of support and referral services for all undergraduate and graduate students who are: veterans currently serving either active duty, Reserve or National Guard; vocational rehabilitation and qualified dependents. These services are tailored to the individual need(s) of each student and can include the following:

  • Assist and advise the student in the application process for admission to the University and obtaining their VA education benefits.
  • Coordinating with the student's academic advisor to ensure they are on the correct track for their career choices
  • Assist students with career choices, counseling and advising
  • Refer students, who need assistance the University does not provide, to local VA resources (e.g., medical, dental, psychological, home loans, etc.)
  • Providing scholarship information and resources
summary: The Veteran Services Office is your liaison to the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs for veteran and veteran-related education benefits and programming activities for student veterans

name: American Marketing Association
description: The American Marketing Association collegiate chapter at UNR offers a platform for professional development for students by providing career resources, experiential learning, and leadership opportunities. AMA also has monthly socials to allow students to connect with each other. UNR AMA serves a group of dynamic, committed students who are interested in Business, Communication, Journalism, Graphic Design and more.
summary: AMA at the University of Nevada, Reno offers a platform for professional development for students who are interested in marketing, journalism or general business.

name: Nevada Student Ambassadors
description: A Nevada Student Ambassador is a University of Nevada, Reno student who is involved with the University’s recruitment and public relations efforts. It is the Student Ambassador’s duty to uphold the high ideals, professionalism, and prestige maintained by the University of Nevada, Reno. In order to become an Ambassador, interested students must fill out an application and be interviewed by current members. There will be a mandatory new member training and tours with current  Ambassadors. Duties as a new Student Ambassador begin upon acceptance of membership,  training, completion of a Director’s Tour, and the attendance at the following retreat.
summary: Nevada Student Ambassadors is an organization that participates in recruitment through tours, Fall Previews, and Admitted Student Receptions. Members are also involved with professional development, social opportunities, and community engagement.

name: University of Nevada, Reno Hawaii Club
description:
We, the people of the University of Nevada Hawaii Club, pledge to share the history of Hawaii by advocating and educating the greater community of Hawaii’s rich culture and heritage.  We will learn about the ancient Hawaiian traditions and values, as well as the modernized way of life on the islands.  Furthermore, it is every member’s duty to honor the established values, to practice them daily and to utilize them in an attempt to better not only our lives, but the lives of our brothers and sisters around us.

In other words, our mission is to provide a home away from home to all!
summary: A home away from home whether that be Hawaii or somewhere far.

name: First Generation Student Center
description:
Our mission is to help students who will be the first in their families to complete a baccalaureate degree, and income-qualified students to overcome class, social and academic barriers to higher education. We believe all students should have an equal opportunity to prepare for, attend and graduate from college.

 

Follow our program instagram accounts for updates: @nevada_trio, @nevadamcnair, @firstinthepack
summary: Helping students overcome barriers to higher education.

name: Omega Delta Phi
description: The purpose of this Brotherhood, a Service/Social fraternity dedicated to the needs and concerns of the community, is and shall be to promote and maintain the traditional values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity, and Leadership. This Brotherhood was founded in order to provide, to ANY man, a diverse fraternal experience which coincides with a higher education.
summary: Multicultural Fraternity dedicated to the needs and concerns of the community. Service/Social

name: The Nevada Sagebrush
description: The Nevada Sagebrush, the student news publication at the University of Nevada, Reno, was established in 1893 and covers all aspects of campus life.
summary: The Nevada Sagebrush, the student news publication at the University of Nevada, Reno, was established in 1893 and covers all aspects of campus life.

name: Wolf Pack Radio
description: Wolf Pack Radio is the only student-run freeform radio station in Northern Nevada, and has been in operation since 1999. WPR gives UNR students a media outlet to express themselves to a wide audience comprising not only of fellow students, but of listeners and readers all over the world through our online stream and partnership with KWNK 97.7FM. All shows are created and broadcast entirely by students. While most shows predominantly feature music, programming addresses a wide range of topics, including politics, sports, and more. WPR provides unique and professional work experience and volunteer opportunities in radio, journalism, and various creative fields to students at the University of Nevada, Reno. No experience is required to become a DJ, and new show applications are accepted at the start of each semester! Tune in anytime at wolfpackradio.org.
summary: Wolf Pack Radio is a student-run, nonprofit radio station, broadcasting from the Joe Crowley Student Union at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: Insight Magazine
description:
The magazine is committed to quality reporting, writing, photography, and design to inform its readers. Insight strives to deliver news in a colorful and captivating way to best tell the stories of people, places, and events of Reno. In addition, the magazine is committed to helping students master their craft through opportunities to develop their skills through print and web mediums.

Check out our most recent issue here!
summary: Insight Magazine is the University of Nevada, Reno’s student-run feature magazine focused on local news, reviews, features, lifestyle, opinions, and our insight on topics. Insight is written by students for students.

name: Brushfire Literature & Arts Journal
description:
The Brushfire Literature & Arts Journal is a student-run organization at the University of Nevada, Reno. Each semester, we publish a journal that showcases new visual art, poetry and short stories made by students and artists at the local level. If you would like to share your art or your writing with us for a chance to be published, please submit your work through our website at www.unrbrushfire.org. The Brushfire exists because of our contributors. Without you, there is no us; no journal to bring together the vast range of voices, styles and ideas at play in the creative community.

With each iteration of the Brushfire, we strive to represent the diversity, originality and dedication of this community. As an ASUN organization, we also strive to be the creative outlet for our student body at UNR.  Our main priority is to connect with the various art communities at UNR and throughout Reno. However, anyone can submit to Brushfire. We receive and publish art from across the country. So, while Brushfire in centered around locality, we seek inclusivity and do not reject art based on the artists’ location.

Brushfire hosts art-oriented events and workshops (e.g. open mics, poetry/writing workshops, guest speakers) throughout the semester. If you are interested in working, volunteering, or getting published in Brushfire — or if you want to help produce the semesterly publication – please at unrbrushfirejournal@gmail.com.
summary: The Brushfire Literature & Arts Journal is a student-run organization at the University of Nevada, Reno. Brushfire publishes biannually. All the art that is reviewed and published by Brushfire is submitted through our website.

name: Reno College Panhellenic Association
description: The Reno College Panhellenic Council strives to promote collaboration and communication throughout the Greek community through its chapters, council, and community. Through this, we will create an impact of academic excellence, leadership, and unity throughout Greek life and campus.
summary: The Reno College Panhellenic Association is comprised of the six Panhellenic Sororities at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: Concrete Canoe
description: Concrete Canoe is an engineering club on campus that constructs a canoe made of concrete to compete in the annual American Society of Civil Engineers National Concrete Canoe Competition. We provide members the opportunity to gain hands-on, practical experience. Members work as a team in a construction environment, designing and applying concrete mix, and executing basic principles of project management. Although the club is engineering-based, students of any discipline are welcome to join.
summary: A competition team under the UNR American Society of Civil Engineers Student Chapter branch of clubs. We construct a canoe made of concrete to compete in both the regional and national conferences held by the American Society of Civil Engineers.

name: University of Nevada Winter Sports Club
description:
FAQ

IKON CODE —-> Will be emailed out after joining the club!

Who is available to get an IKON discount code?

Nevada Winter Sports club members 

When are pass discounts sent out?

Usually within a week from you submitted the form!(during the season)

How do I join the club?

Join our organization on PackLife and pay club dues!

What is the winter sports club about?

We are about having fun out on the mountain and meeting new people while enjoying the sport that we love. There are three different parts of the club. The largest part of the club is the social club which includes pass discounts, a GroupMe for coordinating carpools, and fun events with free food. The freeride team has a competitive emphasis and enables athletes to compete in FWQ's and participate in training events.

How much are club dues?

Social- $40

Freeride- $60
summary: The UNR Winter Sports Club is open to all undergraduate and graduate students at UNR. We welcome skiers and snowboarders of all ability levels. We offer recreational and competitive skiing and snowboarding opportunities. Send us a message to learn more!

name: Japanese Student Action Network
description: JSAN is a cultural exchange club primarily between America and Japan but is open to all cultures from around the world. All our events are geared towards educating members of the Reno and Sparks community on Japanese culture through various activities. Some of these activities can include festivals, holidays and even cooking demos.
summary: JSAN is a cultural exchange club primarily between America and Japan but is open to all cultures from around the world. All our events are geared towards educating members of the Reno and Sparks community on Japanese culture through various activities.

name: Latino Student Advisory Board
description: The purpose of the organization is to contribute to the betterment of the Latino community. The Latino Student Advisory Board aims to unite University faculty, administration, staff members, students, and community leaders to incorporate our ideas and/or suggestions into policy making at our University, state and national level for the empowerment and advancement of the Latino community through public outreach, academic excellence, and civic engagement. The Latino Research Center Student Advisory Board is primarily composed of University students, but also incorporates ideas and suggestions from faculty members and prominent community leaders, who strive for the enhancement of the Hispanic community in Nevada through public outreach, academic excellence and political movements.
summary: The Latino Student Advisory Board aims to incorporate our ideas and/or suggestions into policy making at our University, state, and national level for the empowerment and advancement of the Latino community through public outreach, academic excellence,

name: UNR Spanish Club
summary: The Spanish Club provides all students with educational opportunities to meet outside of the classroom in order to build and develop relationships among them. The Club will function as a link between students & the community in general.

name: No’o Patu Natunetooena
summary: The No'o Patu Natunetooena serves to provide a sense of community for Indigenous students and allies on campus, as well as connecting students to tribal communities, and to educate the general public on Indigenous issues.

name: Every Nation Campus at the University of Nevada, Reno
description: Every Nation campus at the University of Nevada, Reno (ENC @UNR) is a diverse, church-based campus ministry dedicated to providing UNR students with the opportunity to pursue authentic relationships and spiritual growth by offering small group Bible Studies on Campus, a student friendly local church to attend, practical discipleship and ministry opportunities, and much more!
summary: ENC UNR is dedicated to helping college students find authentic relationships and experience spiritual growth.

name: Chinese Students & Scholars Association
description: 我们为即将就读 UNR 或已经就读 UNR 的中国学生和学者提供帮助，帮助他们更好地适应美国的学术和校园生活。
summary: we are the group who is trying to help out the new coming international chinese students to get use to live in America and sharing the Chinese culture with American students.

name: Nevada Electric Racing
description:
The Nevada Electric Racing Club is a competition team whose main goal is to build a fully electric race car that will compete in the Formula SAE Electric competition. Formula SAE (Society of Automotive Engineers) is a student engineering competition that challenges teams to design, build, and compete with small-scale Formula-style race cars. The competition typically includes events such as a design presentation, a cost-analysis presentation, an engineering design report, and on-track performance trials. The goal of the competition is to provide students with a hands-on learning experience that allows them to apply their knowledge of engineering and design principles to a real-world problem.

Our club is open to all college students who are eager to learn and work together towards creating a competitive race car. Whether you're an experienced engineer, a car enthusiast, or simply looking for a unique learning experience, this club is for you! We will provide the resources and support necessary to ensure a successful project. We look forward to having you join us in this exciting endeavor! https://linktr.ee/unr_ner
summary: The Nevada Electric Racing Club is an engineering competition team whose main goal is to design and manufacture a fully electric race car that will compete in the Formula SAE Electric competition.

name: A.B.L.E. Women
description:
Chartered April 1st, 2000, A.B.L.E Women of UNR was the first  is a vibrant,  all inclusive network dedicated to celebrating and advancing women from all backgrounds. Our mission is to provide a supportive and dynamic platform where women of ambition, beauty, leadership, and equality can connect, collaborate, and thrive.

We believe that every woman brings a unique perspective and strength to the table, and our diverse membership reflects this rich tapestry of experiences. Through networking events, leadership workshops, and cultural exchanges, we empower our members to achieve their personal and professional goals while fostering a community built on mutual respect and shared aspirations.

Join us in celebrating the power of diversity and the pursuit of excellence. Whether you're an aspiring leader, a creative visionary, or a champion of equality, the Empowerment Collective is here to support and inspire you on your journey!
summary: We aim to empower women in multicultural backgrounds and to inspire service, scholarship, and leadership amongst our communities.

name: Phi Delta Theta
description:
We are the premiere fraternal organization in Northern Nevada. Founded nationally in 1848 and locally in 1972, we have a proven track record producing men who exemplify the three cardinal principles of Phi Delta Theta: Friendship, Sound Learning, and Moral Rectitude. Some of our alumni from our chapter have gone on to become lawyers, doctors, and Nevada senators.
With a strong brotherhood and a lively chapter house, we're sure you'll find a place in our organization. Join us, and become the greatest version of yourself.
summary: We are the premiere fraternal organization in Northern Nevada. Founded nationally in 1848 and locally in 1972, we have a proven track record producing men who exemplify the three cardinal principles of Phi Delta Theta.

name: Wolf Pack Veterans Club
description:
The goal of Wolf Pack Veterans is to empower student veterans at UNR to reach their academic goals, foster a strong sense of community for veteran and military students, and break down barriers between civilian and military students.

As a member of the Wolf Pack Veterans you will meet other highly motivated students that have a background in military service. Together as members of the Wolf Pack Veterans we will make a stronger community on the Nevada campus. membership is free and anyone can join.
summary: Wolf Pack Vets seeks to create a strong community of students with a military background and break down stigmas that surround the veteran community.

name: Sigma Kappa
description:
Sigma Kappa sisters make a bold commitment every day: we live with heart. To us, this means committing ourselves to one another, the causes we believe in, and a shared dedication to seeking intellectual, spiritual, and social enlightenment throughout our lives.

We are devoted to embodying the values of Sigma Kappa in everything we do. Our promise to exemplify personal growth, service, friendship, and loyalty is what makes Sigma Kappa stand apart. Living with heart makes us more compassionate individuals, leading purposeful lives rooted in a strong sense of character and values, which ultimately makes us who we are.
summary: The purpose of Sigma Kappa Sorority is to provide women lifelong opportunities and support for social, intellectual and spiritual development by bringing women together to positively impact our communities.

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description: The Kappa Alpha Theta, Beta Mu Chapter has been a social sorority at the University of Nevada, Reno since 1922 and is currently home to almost 160 leading women. In Theta, we strive towards spreading the widest influence for good and attaining the highest scholarship. Kappa Alpha Theta provides the love and support system sought by its collegian members as being their home away from home along with lifelong sisterhood. Theta offers a space to foster leadership growth and to prepare our members for the broader demands of mature life after college. Membership entails attending weekly chapter meetings, community service every semester, sisterhood events, social events with other Greek organizations as well as fundraising for our philanthropies, of which we have three: CASA (Court Appointed Special Advocates), The Friendship Fund and the Kappa Alpha Theta Foundation. We pride ourselves of being the first Greek letter fraternity known among women, founded on January 27th, 1870 at DePauw University, Indiana.
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta- Beta Mu is a social sorority founded at the University of Nevada, Reno in 1922. Our aim is to foster lifelong sisterhood, strive to spread the widest influence for good and to obtain the highest scholarship.

name: MEDLIFE Nevada
description: MEDLIFE Nevada is a volunteer organization that emphasizes medicine, education, and development. As a club, we prioritize service that benefits both the local Reno community as well as the international community. We work closely with the International MEDLIFE chapter to provide unique opportunities and events to our members. On-campus, we work with our members to plan events, volunteer together, and create a supportive and fun environment!
summary: MEDLIFE Nevada is a volunteer organization that emphasizes medicine, education, and development. As a club, we prioritize service that benefits both the local Reno community as well as the international community.

name: Public Health Coalition
description: The Public Health Coalition will focus on pre-professional preparedness and community outreach. It will get the UNR undergraduate student population involved with the public health community, pre-professional networking, and spreading health awareness.
summary: The Public Health Coalition will focus on pre-professional preparedness and community outreach. It will get the UNR undergraduate student population involved with the public health community, pre-professional networking, and spreading health awareness.

name: Business Student Council
description:
The Business Student Council is made up of College of Business students from each class, freshman through senior level.

Benefits include direct access and advisement to the Dean, resume building experiences including leadership positions, increased opportunities to interact with business professionals and the community at both college and community events, and the opportunity to be seen in a professional light as an ambassador of the College of Business.
summary: The Business Student Council advises the Dean and creates opportunities in alumni engagement, professional development, community service, student involvement, professional travel, socials and networking.

name: Management and Human Resources Association
description: The University of Nevada, Reno Management and Human Resources Association is dedicated to expanding students’ exposure to career-oriented experiences and opportunities through student networking, leadership roles, and guest speakers.
summary: UNR MHRA is dedicated to expanding students’ exposure to career-oriented experiences and opportunities through student networking, leadership roles, and guest speakers.  The focus of this organization is Human Resources and Management disciplines.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
The Mission of the ASCE-AGC Student Chapter at the University of Nevada, Reno is to
Provide hands-on experience using classroom concepts network with industry, transition students to professionals, educate the community about engineering, and encourage lifelong learning and membership in ASCE and AGC
summary: We provide hands-on opportunities to apply your engineering knowledge, networking opportunities with professionals, and prepare members for the transition from student to professionals.

name: Wolf Pack Racing
description:
Wolf Pack Racking operates as a Baja SAE team, involving students in the comprehensive process of designing, manufacturing, and racing a single-seat off-road car, competing with colleges globally. Participation is not limited by major.

Regular club meetings occur weekly, and members convene on weekends for manufacturing sessions and drive days. Additionally, the team participates in a yearly Baja SAE race event.
summary: Wolf Pack Racking is a club where the students design, manufacture, and race a single-seat off-road race car against other colleges worldwide.

name: Order of Omega
summary: Order of Omega consists of fraternity and sorority life members who excel as students, leaders, community members, and siblings in their organizations. It is an organization to gather together increasing collaboration and unity in Greek life on campus.

name: Pre-PA Society Club
summary: The mission of the Pre-PA Society is to familiarize students with the Physician Assistant profession and the admission process through seminars, guest speakers, hands-on experience, critical thinking, and community service/engagement opportunities.

name: Nevada Latin Dance Club
description: N.L.D.C. is here to promote the teaching of Latin dancing, to spread the enthusiasm of Latin culture to other students, and to organize social events to connect with others. Various styles like Salsa, Merengue, Bachata, Cumbia and Reggaetón. Come enjoy the fun y bailemos! (let's dance!) :)
summary: N.L.D.C. is here to promote the teaching of Latin dancing, to spread the enthusiasm of Latin culture to other students, and to organize social events to connect with others. Various styles like Salsa, Merengue, Bachata, and Cumbia. Come enjoy the fun :)

name: CABNR Ambassadors
summary: Assist incoming students with scheduling, finding classes, and making degree choices. We help incoming freshmen with their transition and provide any additional information/experiences to aid them in being a successful student at UNR.

name: Honors College
description: The mission of the Honors College is to build a community of students, faculty, staff, and external stakeholders to provide enriching educational experiences that exemplify a commitment to resilience, ingenuity, stewardship, and equity.
summary: At the University of Nevada, Reno Honors College, our students maximize their unique gifts and passions through a series of personalized experiences that deepen their capacity for transformative societal impact.

name: Nevada Loggers
description:
America has a deep-rooted logging history that goes back to the 1600s, and the tradition is being carried on by members of the Nevada Loggers

The Nevada Loggers provides students of all majors the opportunity to learn collegiate timber sports skills such as chopping, sawing, throwing and climbing, and then test their skills in competitions.
summary:
America has a deep-rooted logging history that goes back to the 1600s, and the tradition is being carried on by members of the Nevada Loggers

The Nevada Loggers provides students of all majors the opportunity to learn collegiate timber sports skills.

name: UNR Best Buddies
summary: Best Buddies International is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to establishing a global volunteer movement that creates opportunities for one-to-one friendships, integrated employment, leadership development, and inclusive living.

name: Bollywood America
summary: Bollywood America is a performance based club, that will be primarily dance based. The goal of this club is to learn and promote bollywood and Indian culture, and allow students to engage with the culture whilst having fun.

name: Recovery Rising
description: The mission of Pack for Recovery is to promote and support academic interests, professional development, community engagement, leadership skills, of students and the support of Nevada's Recovery and Prevention (NRAP) by spreading awareness about resources on campus for students who want to support others in recovery.
summary: The mission of Pack for Recovery is to promote and support academic interests, professional development, community engagement, leadership skills, for students at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: Gamma Rho Lambda
description:
Gamma Rho Lambda National Sorority is the first all-inclusive, LGBTQ+, college-based sorority with chapters throughout the United States.

Our goal is to provide the infrastructure necessary for our chapters, provisional chapters, and members to grow and prosper.
summary: GRL is an LGBTQ+-based sorority, accepting members of all gender identities.

name: oSTEM at Nevada
description: The purpose of oSTEM is to unite and support queer students and allies during their undergraduate and graduate studies of a STEM topic, and providing a continued network support to queer students into their STEM-focused careers. Meetings focus on a variety of themes, including academic support, career building, community service, and more.
summary: The purpose of oSTEM is to unite and support queer students and allies during their undergraduate and graduate studies of a STEM topic, and providing a continued network support to queer students into their STEM-focused careers.

name: Pi Tau Sigma Beta Nu
description: Our mission is to strive to create better engineers through commitment to academic excellence and dedication to service. Pi Tau Sigma prides itself in the core values: Integrity (soundness of character and moral conduct), Service (serving the mechanical engineering profession and the community), and Leadership (fostering initiative through example in a professional manner).
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is a Mechanical Engineering Honors Society which values and promotes integrity and leadership through academic, community, and professional service opportunities.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Student Branch Chapter of the University of Nevada, Reno
summary: The purpose of IEEE will be the dissemination of knowledge and experience through the theory and practice of all aspects of electrical and associated engineering fields, as well as enhanced student professional development.

name: Nevada Cyber Club
description:
Our objective is to teach cyber skills to students and provide community outreach that will connect members to jobs in the field of Cyber Security (and Cyber in general) upon graduation.

As a club, we are dedicated to conducting research that will help to make advancements in policy and serve to better inform our community and the cyber world at large.

 

Join our Discord here

 

To become an active member, follow the steps outlined in our Discord.

For non-UNR members/alumni please contact an officer in our Discord directly.
summary: Our objective is to teach cyber skills to students and provide community outreach that will connect members to jobs in the field of Cyber Security (and Cyber in general) upon graduation.

name: Nevada Microbiology Club
description: The Nevada Microbiology Club is a space for individuals interested in the microbes that surround us. Every week, we practice various techniques used in research, clinical laboratories, and practical settings, where we teach in a way for all students to learn and enjoy. We work with relatively safe microbes, ensuring student safety and teaching aseptic technique. Additionally, we offer field trips to explore the forefront of the microbiology field and have guest speakers share their experiences and wisdom. For students in the life sciences or those taking microbiology classes and labs, we are a great resource for getting ahead. We are also a low-commitment club, where students can pick and choose what meetings they'd like to show up for. We are open to all majors and encourage others to give us a try!
summary: Microbiology Club will engage its members in activities to promote their scientific and professional development as well as being a resource for members to connect with the microbiology community.

name: UNR Astronomy Club
description: The UNR Astronomy Club is a welcoming and close community. We enjoy time together discussing astronomy, stargazing, participating in community star parties, and game nights together. We’ve had guest speaker presentations in the past and we have had the opportunity to meet individuals of the Discover Science Lecture series, such as Gabriella Gonzales. We are working to incorporate community outreach, especially with the university planetarium and local schools. Several members are skilled in astrophotography and love to share their photos and techniques with the rest of the club. We additionally have remote access to the Great Basin Observatory (GBO) and are looking forward to virtual telescope viewings through GBO and Slooh telescopes.
summary: The UNR Astronomy Club consists of a group of motivated and curious students who wish to share the wonders of the night sky with each other and with the local community.

name: Nevada Climbing
description:
Nevada Climbing offers group practices two times per week, training with student and guest coaches, outdoor excursions, safety clinics, and access to competitions with teams from other universities via USA Climbing. 

Financial commitment to the team provides members:

     -Discounts at the local climbing gyms (Basecamp Reno, Mesa Rim Reno, The Block Climbing and Fitness, North Peak Climbing)

     -Team apparel

     -Access to team-owned gear for indoor and outdoor climbing

     -Competitive members receive funding of USA Climbing membership and travel expenses

Dues to join Nevada Climbing are $40/ semester or $60/year.

To join the competitive team, dues are an extra $35 ($95 total).

Please contact us via email (NevadaClimbing@gmail.com) or Instagram DM (@NevadaClimbing) if you are interested in joining or have any questions we can answer!
summary: Nevada Climbing is an organization devoted to promoting safety and proficiency in the sport of rock climbing. We offer community and support for climbers of all levels, all with the goal of expanding the sport of climbing at UNR.

name: RenoACDA
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to advance vocal and choral music by providing resources and opportunities to develop students as professional musicians, leaders, educators and conductors.

name: Pre-Pharmacy Club
summary: To help inform students about the Pharmacy field and how to be the best applicant

name: Nevada Pre-Optometry Association
summary: NPOA is a student run organization that allows individuals interested in the field of Optometry to come together to learn and prepare for Optometry School. It allows for a small, supportive community during the process of applying to school.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
description: Our club focuses on serving our community and developing skills needed for professional school. Our club is open to any students who are pursuing professional or graduate school in any health-related field which includes medical and dental school, physical therapy programs, and even nursing students interested in pursuing a graduate degree.
summary: Hello, we are Alpha Epsilon Delta and we are a pre-professional health national honor society on campus. Our club focuses on serving our community and developing skills needed for professional school.

name: Psi Chi
description:
Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, was founded in 1929 with a mission of recognizing and promoting excellence in the science and application of psychology. Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology. 

All Psi Chi memberships are for life.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hzhkgwo9byo
summary: Psi Chi is an honors society that recognizes and promotes the excellence in the science and application of psychology. Membership is open to students who are majoring or minoring in psychology. We are also paired with the Psychology Club.

name: American Medical Student Association
description: The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is a pre-medicine club committed to assisting, guiding, and supporting students on their path to medicine. We strive to create a general framework for our members to build off of and/or adjust to their specific needs, along with a foundation of pre-medical resources for our members to thrive from. Such resources include guest speakers, volunteer opportunities, information sessions, workshops, MCAT practice, mock interviews, clinical experience opportunities, and, most importantly, social events. Providing a place for students of similar interests to connect and interact is vital to the success of our members and our club as a whole. Our overall goal is to continue molding and shaping our members to be better prepared than ever before through constant effort, engagement, and communication.
summary: The American Medical Student Association (AMSA) is a pre-medicine club committed to assisting, guiding, and supporting students on their path to medicine.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students
description: MAPS is a national organization that aims to provide underrepresented pre-health students with knowledge, skills and experience that are both prerequisite and concomitant to professional participation in health care fields. We also aim to improve minority student matriculation into all professional health related programs, with emphasis on medicine. Lastly, we aim to encourage culturally conscious physicians by increasing awareness of issues that underrepresented communities face.
summary: MAPS is a national organization that aims to provide underrepresented pre-health students with knowledge, skills and experience that are both prerequisite and concomitant to professional participation in health care fields.

name: Nevada Men's Lacrosse
description: We are the Nevada Men's Lacrosse team. We play in the North Division of the Western Collegiate Lacrosse League (WCLL) in the Major Collegiate Lacrosse League (MCLA). The WCLL North Division consists of the California Golden Bears, Dominican Penguins, Sonoma State Seawolves, and of course your Nevada Wolf Pack. Our team works to compete at the highest level and win a MCLA Division 1 National Championship.
summary: We are the Nevada Men's Lacrosse team.

name: Nevada Mock Trial Team
description: Nevada Mock Trial is a competitive, pre-professional team composed of University of Nevada, Reno students. During the first semester, the team receives a nationally recognized, fictitious case from the American Mock Trial Association (AMTA). Members participate in courtroom procedures through competitive trials, which fluctuate yearly between criminal and civil proceedings. The team travels throughout the West Coast for scrimmages, invitationals, and competitions, including Regionals. NVMT is a recognized team by the University of Nevada, Reno, and its Associated Student Union and won "Best New Club" in 2016.
summary: The UNR Mock Trial Team is a competitive club that receives fictional criminal and civil cases and prepares those cases as both the attorneys and witnesses. Both semesters team members their case occasionally competing against other schools nationwide.

name: Student Trauma Intervention Program
description:
Trauma Intervention Programs (TIP) is a national non-profit organization of volunteers dedicated to ensuring that those who are emotionally traumatized in emergency situations receive the assistance they need. To accomplish that goal, TIP Inc. works closely with local communities to establish emergency services volunteer programs. In these programs, well-trained citizen volunteers are called to emergency scenes to assist family members, witnesses, and other bystanders whom the emergency system often must leave behind.

What TIP Volunteers do:

  • Provide emotional support.
  • Help arrange for shelter, food, clothing, and transportation.
  • Notify family and friends.
  • Serve as liaison between the victims and emergency and hospital authorities.
  • Help arrange follow-up services.
  • Provide information and referrals to appropriate agencies for ongoing assistance.

Following a traumatic event, the person involved often feels helpless, confused, and in emotional shock. They are confronted with a situation for which they are totally unprepared. Often there is no one available in the immediate aftermath of a tragedy to guide the victims through these difficult hours. The TIP volunteer provides this necessary temporary support until persons involved in the crisis are able to depend on family, friends, neighbors, and other community resources.

TIP volunteers are always available. They are called by authorized police officers, firefighters, paramedics, hospital personnel, and business and administrative leaders to assist family members and friends following a natural, accidental, or unexpected death (including suicide and SIDS).
summary: This organization is committed to raise awareness about the importance of helping emotionally traumatized citizens. Also, it is devoted to attracting and training more potential members to comfort and support survivors of tragic events. 39 service hrs/mo

name: Phi Delta Epsilon - Nevada Alpha Chapter
description:
Phi Delta Epsilon is an international medical fraternity.  We are the largest and oldest co-ed medical fraternity in the world, spanning hundreds of chapters and thousands of members across the world. Gaining membership in Phi Delta Epsilon is highly competitive and representative of many traits that we value in our future physicians: well-rounded, astute, determined, social, and exceptionally capable. As Phi Delta Epsilon, we mobilize our members to volunteer and engage with the community on public health issues. This fraternity not only helps our members develop professionally, but also helps our members create close, personal bonds with each other. 

We are the Nevada Alpha undergraduate chapter at the University of Nevada, Reno, founded in 2012, with the advisorship of Gina Sella. Through our passion and dedication towards excellence in the field of medicine, we have expanded tremendously and initiated many incredibly talented new members since our inception. Our alumni have been accepted and matriculated to prestigious medical institutions such as UC Davis, UNSOM, and UCSC medical schools. We pride ourselves on having an acceptance rate to medical schools much higher than the average University of Nevada, Reno premedical student.
summary: The primary vision of Phi Delta Epsilon medical fraternity is to develop physicians who positively impact the world, inspire others to greatness, and lead by example.

name: Turning Point USA
description: Turning Point USA’s mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and capitalism through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.
summary: Turning Point USA’s mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and capitalism through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a national co-ed professional service fraternity. Its purpose is to develop leadership, promote friendship and provide service to humanity. More than 500,000 students on 375 college campuses have chosen Alpha Phi Omega, making it the nation’s largest Greek letter fraternity. 

The Upsilon Psi chapter at the University of Nevada, Reno was chartered on May 28, 2018. Since then, we strive to exemplify the ideals of leadership, friendship, and service. We offer workshops and various officer positions to grow as leaders, host brotherhood events to strengthen our connections with one another and provide volunteering opportunities both on and off campus to serve our fraternity, campus, community, and nation.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega is a co-ed national service fraternity founded on the principles of leadership, friendship, and service. We aim to develop leadership skills, form lifelong friendships, and serve the community around us.

name: Undergraduate Student Social Work Association
summary: Committed to bringing the social work's values and ideals alive through interactions with one another, the University, the School and the community through volunteerism and advocacy.

name: Blue Key Honor Society
summary: Blue Key Honor Society, founded November 27, 1924, is a premier honor society that recognizes college students at senior institutions of higher education for balanced and all-around excellence in scholarship, leadership, and service.

name: Nu Rho Psi
summary: Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society for neuroscience majors/minors. Nu Rho Psi is the only nationally recognized honor society in the area of brain research. We are the Nevada Chapter and primarily focus on excellence, scholarship and service.

name: Nevada Circle K International
description:
We are UNR's Circle K club and a part of the Kiwanis family. Our core tenets are service, leadership, and fellowship.

Wish Key Club lasted longer than high school? Come to Circle K!
summary: We promote service, fellowship, and leadership.

name: Musical Therapy Club
summary: The Musical Therapy Club aims to incorporate values of professionalism, self-improvement, collaboration, and community for the purpose of providing philanthropy, primarily through the medium of music.

name: Student Organ Donation Advocates
description: While 95% of U.S. adults support organ donation only 54% are actual registered donors. Many myths and conceptions stop individuals from registering. SODA at the University of Nevada, Reno aims to educate our campus and Reno community on these misconceptions and create lifelong organ donation ambassadors. Our efforts include advocacy tabling, discussions with members of the donation community, donation kit creation, and much more!
summary: SODA is a non-profit national organization that inspires and supports student-led organ donation education and registration efforts through high school and college chapters.

name: Camp Kesem at University of Nevada, Reno
description: The purpose of Camp Kesem is to provide leadership and volunteer opportunities for the students of the University of Nevada, Reno while serving the Reno/Sparks/Tahoe community and greater metropolitan area. Camp Kesem recognizes and embraces the often-overlooked population of children affected by a parent’s cancer. A one-week-long summer camp will be organized each year for children 6 to 18 years old whose parent or primary caregiver has had or currently has cancer. No other type of child may participate in the camp. Camp kesem will be a place where these children can experience the simple joys of childhood and relate to other children in similar situations. Student leaders of Camp Kesem will organize all aspects of the camp and fundraise enough money for every child who attends the camp to do so for free.
summary:
To provide free summer camps and year-round peer support for children impacted by a
parent’s cancer in the Reno/Sparks community and surrounding areas.

name: Delta Delta Delta Sorority
description:
The Theta Theta Chapter at University of Nevada, Reno was founded in 1913 by Sarah Ida Shaw as the first Greek organization in the state of Nevada. We are now lucky to say, we are a strong chapter of 160 brave, bold and true collegiate members eager to continue indulging in everything sorority life has to offer. Tri Delta teaches great truth and sacrifice through leadership roles and the meaning of perpetual bonds of friendship that are capable of lasting a lifetime. 

Theta Theta has the honor of fundraising for St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, our nation wide philanthropy, through organized events like Delta House of Pancakes, Triple Play, 10k in One Day and we’re excited to announce we will be conducting a new event called Delta Hold ‘Em in the Spring of 2022. We promote sisterhood through participation in these events, as well as several others including our annual themed New Member Dance, Big/Little, All House and Officer Retreats, open chapter development meetings and several other sisterhood social events. 

We strive to participate in the Greek community by taking part in Homecoming and other Greek philanthropy events. In the Fall of 2021, we will be conducting our first joint ice cream themed philanthropy event, Triple Skoop, with a neighboring sorority Sigma Kappa and we will also be working with Alpha Tau Omega, a fraternity on campus, to put on our third annual haunted house event. 

Theta Theta is a chapter of confident, motivated students of the University of Nevada, Reno proud to be called Tri Delta women.
summary: Theta Theta is made up of brave, bold and true collegiate members eager to continue indulging in everything sorority life has to offer. Tri Delta teaches great strength and sacrifice through leadership roles and the true meaning of lifelong friendships.

name: UNR National Association for Music Education Collegiate
description:
NAfME is the professional organization for all music educators - collegiate, active, and retired

 

As a member of NAfME Collegiate, you will:

  • Gain the benefit of a professional identity at the earliest stages of your career,
  • Have exclusive opportunities to interact with and learn from active music educators and administrators,
  • Receive valuable professional development at state and national conferences at a significantly reduced cost (or free),
  • Participate in activities and programs that will prepare you for your career, and
  • Have networking opportunities that will help you get job interviews and land a job.

Feel free to contact our chapter via email for more information!
summary: UNR National Association for Music Education Collegiate is a club that offers Music Education majors the opportunity to learn more about our field, connect with the community, organize events and build relationships within the club.

name: Queer and LGBT Advocacy Board
summary: The Queer and LGBT Advocacy Board (QLAB), comprised of out and allied faculty and staff, makes ongoing assessment of attitudes and conditions throughout the University regarding gay, lesbian, bisexual, trans and Queer persons and issues.

name: UNR Men's Rugby
summary: The Men's rugby team of UNR has been a part of the university for over 100 years, and we continue to establish brotherhood through the amazing sport of rugby.

name: NevadaTeach STEM Club
description: NevadaTeach helps students gain two majors, a degree in STEM and a degree in education. This club celebrates both majors as one and together. Students can come together to talk about new scientific concepts, and education majors have the ability to talk about how they would want to teach these concepts to their own learners while getting feedback from their peers. This is a club about making connections and friendships within your major's community.
summary: A place where STEM students are able to come together and voice their ideas and create new friendships. Also a place for students pursuing STEM teaching degrees can create connections for their future classrooms.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: We are a group of students mainly focused around chemical engineering but welcoming to all majors. The club serves as the Nevada Chapter for the national AIChE organization. We seek to help get more people interested in the field of chemical engineering.

name: The UNR Film Club
description: The UNR Film Club is dedicated to help inspire young artist create their voice through the art of filmmaking. In addition, we want to connect our members to those within the filmmaking community and find career development opportunities.
summary: The UNR Film Club is dedicated to help inspire young artist create their voice through the art of filmmaking. In addition, we want to connect our members to those within the filmmaking community and find career development opportunities.

name: Orvis Student Nurses Association
description:
OSNA’s mission is to better our local community through charities, philanthropic events, and health promotion. Our purpose is to assume responsibility for contributing to nursing education in order to provide for the highest quality health care, to provide programs representative of fundamental and current professional interests and concerns, and to aid in the development of the whole person, the professional role and their responsibility for the health care of people in all walks of life. 

We function to have direct input into standards of nursing education and influence the educational process, as well as influencing health care, nursing education and practice through legislative activities as appropriate. We promote and encourage participation in community affairs and activities towards improved health care and the resolution of related social issues. Additionally, we represent nursing students to the consumer, to institutions and other organizations, and promote collaborative relationships with the American Nurses Association, the National League for Nursing and the International Council of Nurses.
summary: OSNA’s mission is to better our local community through charities, philanthropic events, and health promotion.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International
description:
Phi Alpha Delta is the University's only pre-law professional organization which strives to provide undergraduate students with a well rounded experience through workshops, attorney and law school visits, volunteer opportunities and social events.

P.A.D. UNR practices open recruitment and does not discriminate based on sex, sexual orientation, race, gender identity, socio-economic status, religion, creed, etc. Students may pay a one time due payment to the National Chapter of PAD if they wish to gain membership into the national chapter.
summary: P.A.D. is UNR's only pre-law professional organization and strives to provide students with well rounded preparation for a potential legal career with workshops, attorney and law school visits, and community service opportunities.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary:
The UNR chapter of the society of women engineers provides a platform for professional
networking, youth outreach, and advocacy for gender equality in engineering.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi
description:
Alpha Epsilon Pi is a Jewish fraternity, and brotherhood in Alpha Epsilon Pi is open to any Jewish and non-Jewish men who are willing to espouse its values and mission: developing future leaders in the Jewish community through daily acts of brotherhood, charity, social awareness, and support for Jewish communities. The Nevada chapter, Upsilon Nu, prides itself in our strong brotherly connections, community engagement, and active local alumni.

We are our brothers' keeper.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Pi is a fraternity open to any Jewish or non-Jewish men who are willing to espouse its values and mission: developing leaders through daily acts of brotherhood, charity, social awareness, and support for Jewish communities.

name: Sigma Omega Nu, Latina Interest Sorority Inc.
summary: Sigma Omega Nu is a Latina Interest Sorority but not exclusive. We welcome ladies of all backgrounds and cultures. Our main focus is academics, cultura, and sisterhood. We also strive to help our community in any way we can.

name: Phi Sigma Rho Sorority
description: Phi Sigma Rho is a social sorority for STEM majors. Our goal is to provide all the benefits of greek life while accommodating the busy schedule of a woman or non-binary person in STEM. We focus on building connections between sisters as well as promoting academic and professional success through the support of our sisters around the world.
summary: Phi Sigma Rho was founded with the vision to be the foremost social sorority for women in STEM by fostering among its members' lifelong friendship, scholarship, and encouragement. Our mission is in Re-Engineering Sorority Life!

name: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority Inc.
description: The purpose of Kappa Delta Chi shall be to promote the tradition values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity and Leadership of its members. A organization dedicated to service for institutions of higher education and underrepresented communities with special emphasis on the Hispanic/Lation population; a servitude based on love and friendship.
summary: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority is a national sorority who aims to achieve professional development, academic excellence, and graduation of all its members; an organization dedicated to service with an emphasis on the Hispanic/Latino population.

name: Alpha Omicron Pi
summary: Alpha Omicron Pi is a member of the National Panhellenic Conference (NPC), an umbrella term for the 26 inter/national women's fraternities and sororities. Alpha Omicron Pi joined the NPC in 1905, entering as the 12th organization.

name: Lambda Psi Rho
description: Lambda Psi Rho is an Asian-interest, but not Asian-exclusive, organization at the University of Nevada, Reno. We continuously promote brotherhood and diversity, foster leadership, succeed academically, and serve the community.
summary: Lambda Psi Rho is an Asian-interest, but not Asian-exclusive, organization at the University of Nevada, Reno. We continuously promote brotherhood and diversity, foster leadership, succeed academically, and serve the community.

name: Nu Alpha Kappa Fraternity, Inc
description: Our pillars Cultura, Academics, and Brotherhood give our organization the foundation to succeed as college students. Being a Latino based fraternity culture is very important, and we encompass all cultures nationally and locally. Being a college student you should have one goal in mind, to graduate! We have a huge support system in the Reno area, and we pride ourselves with the education we receive here at they University of Nevada-Reno campus. Our organization believes in strong brotherhood, and that begins with our active chapter, and our local Alumni chapter. Together we form Nu Alpha Kappa, inc. a fraternity that comes together and believe in strong relations in college and many years after.
summary: We, NU ALPHA KAPPA, seek to unite and involve all students in a more harmonious and brotherly atmosphere through academic, social and cultural means.

name: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority Inc.
description: Kappa Phi Lambda is an Asian-interest but not Asian-exclusive sorority whose three pillars are sisterhood, service, and cultural diversity. The Alpha Theta Chapter of Kappa Phi Lambda was founded on May 2, 2015. Kappa Phi Lambda is one of the fastest growing Asian-American interest sororities in the United States.
summary: Kappa Phi Lambda is an Asian-interest but not Asian-exclusive sorority whose three pillars are sisterhood, service, and cultural diversity. The Alpha Theta Chapter of Kappa Phi Lambda was founded on May 2, 2015.

name: Nevada Men's Soccer
description: The Nevada Men's Soccer team is a competitive club soccer team. Every semester we compete against other universities club soccer teams in a league format. Beyond standard league play we aim to compete in one tournament per semester as a team goal. Our club offers a chance to play competitive soccer for those who want to stay involved in the game, as well as a great community and sports culture for our students.
summary: We are a competitive club soccer team that competes primarily in California. We invite players of all levels to come to tryouts!

name: Nevada Glee Club
description: Glee Club is a low-pressure, welcoming space for anyone who enjoys music and singing. Weekly meetings primarily consist of casual, open-mic style karaoke. Events such as themed socials, community service events, and performances may be held periodically throughout the year.
summary: Nevada Glee Club aims to foster camaraderie through shared musical experience, including performances, social interaction, and community service. Other goals of the club include musical education and showcasing the talents of its members.

name: Wolf Pack Swing Dance Club
description: The mission of the WPSDC is to promote, present, and teach swing dance to the student body of the University of Nevada, Reno, present opportunities to participate in swing dancing in a safe, comfortable, and fun environment, and to educate the student body and surrounding community about swing and vernacular jazz dance and music.
summary: In the Wolf Pack Swing Dance Club, we learn about and teach swing dances including Lindy Hop, Charleston, Balboa, and Collegiate Shag as well as vintage jazz music, and swing dance history.

name: Dragon Club of Nevada
description:
We want to provide the resources, space, and knowledge in order for members of the club to be able to enjoy and participate in table top games both inside and outside of the club.

Our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/VDN7Pqe
summary:
Join us for Dungeons & Dragons, Pathfinder, or other tabletop games. Prior experience is not necessary.  All are welcome!
Meetings are every Friday at 5:30pm in WRB 2030 during the spring and fall semesters.

name: Pre-Student Osteopathic Medical Association
description: We are currently partnering with a local osteopathic physician in Reno who owns his very own private practice in integrative/alternative medicine. Through this partnership, we are honored to be shadowing this private practice as well as working with other local physicians in the Reno/Sparks area. In addition, Pre-SOMA receives insight regarding state legislative affairs, joining state medical conferences, and other mini field trips.
summary: UNR Pre-SOMA is a newly found medical club focusing specifically on Osteopathic Medicine through recruitment, education, and outreach to osteopathic physicians in the community.

name: Honors College Student Council
description: The Honors College Student Council is an inclusive group that aims to foster a positive and nurturing environment to members of the Honors College, to act as a liaison between Honors College students and staff/faculty, and to provide an atmosphere conducive to intensified learning for motivated students.
summary: The Honors College Student Council is an inclusive group that aims to foster a positive and nurturing environment to members of the Honors College.

name: Nevada Women's Ultimate Frisbee
