summary: Nevada Women’s Ultimate Frisbee participates competitively in Ultimate tournaments throughout the country. Our focus is to improve as individuals, as well as a team. We strive to form lasting friendships with other members within Ultimate Frisbee.

name: 3D Printing Club
description: The 3D Printing Club aims to teach others about maker culture through a variety of student projects and hands-on activities, including 3D printing, crafting, cosplay, robotics, woodworking, electronics, programming, and 3D modeling. This goal includes teaching members how to use various tools and machines, design models and parts for different fabrication processes, and work on collaborative projects. The club provides students with important knowledge about design philosophies, engineering techniques, and how to translate concepts into reality. Through collaborative projects, skill-building sessions, and networking events, the 3D Printing Club cultivates a vibrant community of makers passionate about exploring new technologies and crafting innovative solutions. These concepts can be applied to academic courses and provide valuable experience for career paths in engineering and related fields. Whether you are a seasoned maker or a curious beginner, join us to bring your ideas to life, discover, create, and grow!
summary: The 3D Printing Club teaches maker culture through hands-on activities, providing knowledge on using tools, designing models, and working on collaborative projects. Join us to bring your ideas to life!

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Zeta Iota
description: Zeta Iota is a chapter of Beta Alpha Psi, an honorary accounting society, at the University of Nevada, Reno. Membership in the Zeta Iota chapter gives our members access to essential tools to gain experience in their field of interest, while also providing the best opportunities to network with their peers and potential employers both socially and professionally. Our goal at Beta Alpha Psi is to bridge the gap between the classroom and the workplace. We do this through professional lunches, firm tours, social activities, Meet the Firms and Spring Banquet. Over the past years, Zeta Iota has brought valued financial professionals to the business world while creating priceless memories for its participants.
summary: Zeta Iota is a chapter of Beta Alpha Psi, an honorary accounting society. Membership in the Zeta Iota chapter gives our members access to essential tools to gain experience in their field of interest.

name: Association for Computing Machinery
description:
Hey there! If you're a UNR student, sign in to PackLife above, then click "Join" below!

 

Please join our Discord at discord.gg/KjQ5Yf5MQx! You'll get the most up-to-date announcements from us there.
summary: ACM's mission is to fuel passionate Computer Science students at UNR with opportunities to learn, network, and apply their knowledge in the real world.

name: Nevada Men's Club Volleyball
description: Competitive volleyball club that participates in the Northern California Collegiate Volleyball League (NCCVL) that is a part of the (National Collegiate Volleyball League (NCVL). We support both an A Team who plays in Division 1and a B Team that plays in Division 2.
summary: Nevada Men's Club Volleyball

name: Nevada Shotgun Sports Club
summary: We are a club that participates and competes in shotgun sports such as trap, skeet, and sporting clays events.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
description: • The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is the foremost organization for students interested in public relations and communications. We seek to advance the public relations profession by nurturing generations of future professionals. We advocate rigorous academic standards for public relations education, the highest ethical principles and diversity in the profession.
summary: PRSSA connects you to a network of peers and professionals who will become lifelong friends and mentors. PRSSA facilitates the development of valuable relationships with future colleagues through PRSSA National/ regional events and PRSSA Chapters.

name: Club Tennis
description:
Did you play tennis in high school and want to continue recreationally in college? Have you never played tennis, but want to learn? Look no further, the tennis club is for you!

We are a nationally recognized chapter of the United States Tennis Association (USTA) under the Tennis On Campus program. Each semester, we travel to compete in tournaments across the country—in places like California, Arizona, Texas, and beyond. Not interested in competing? No problem! We host practices where you can improve your skills, stay active, and connect with others who share your love for tennis.

Registration is now closed for the Fall season. Contact Ryan to be added to our list for the Spring. Happy slicing 🐺
summary:
To play tennis, have fun, create meaningful connections 
among tennis enthusiasts of the University, and improve mental and physical health 
on campus.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse
description: Nevada Women’s Lacrosse is a group of players of all levels who aim to grow academically and athletically, enjoy playing the sport, and promote it as pioneers in Northern Nevada.
summary: Nevada Women’s Lacrosse is a group of players with an ambition for academic and athletic growth, to enjoy playing the sport, and to promote lacrosse as pioneers of the sport in Northern Nevada.

name: Women's Club Volleyball
summary: Hi! We are the Nevada women's club volleyball team and we compete in the Northern California Collegiate Club Volleyball League (NCCVL) in Division 1AA. The NCCVL D1AA consists of Cal Poly, Sonoma State, St. Mary's, San Jose State, UC Davis, and Nevada.

name: Nevada Taekwondo Club
description:
The Nevada Taekwondo club primarily offers training in accordance with the official Kukkiwon curriculum, which allows students to earn worldwide-accepted official certifications in Olympic-style Taekwondo. The club trains regularly throughout the week and participates in official tournaments run by the National Collegiate Taekwondo Association (NCTA). Nevada Taekwondo Club is also affiliated with the World Taekwondo Federation (WTF), an international federation governing the sport of Taekwondo and is a member of the Association of Summer Olympic International Federations (ASOIF).
Join our discord for more information at linktr.ee/unr_tkd_club
summary: The Nevada Taekwondo club primarily offers training in accordance with the official Kukkiwon curriculum, which allows students to earn worldwide-accepted official certifications in Olympic-style Taekwondo. More info at discord: linktr.ee/unr_tkd_club

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description: The Purpose of the Chapter is to establish and advance at the University of Nevada, Reno witnessing communities of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God's Word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture, and God's purposes in the world.
summary: We exist to see faculty and students transformed, campuses renewed, and world-changers developed.

name: Nevada Pre-Dental Society
description: The mission of our club is to educate and familiarize interested students in the field of dentistry while helping them prepare for an entrance into dental school. We also aim to build strong relationships with the local dental community as well as neighboring dental schools.
summary: The University of Nevada Reno Pre-Dental Society is a student run organization that strives to prepare undergraduate students for the field of dentistry. Our main goal is to aid students enter their dream dental school.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Professional Co-ed Business Fraternity founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders. Originally founded by 4 men at New York University, the professional organization now has over 298,000 initiated members at 219 universities in 4 countries. Alpha Kappa Psi has grown to be the oldest and largest business fraternity in the world! With an extensive network of alumni, members are able to land opportunities at some of the most profitable companies in the world. As one of the best professional co-ed business fraternities, we are truly preparing our brothers for a life of success.
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Professional Co-ed Business Fraternity founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders.

name: Undergraduate Behavior Analysis Club
description:
The mission of the Undergraduate BA Club is to promote and support academic interests and professional development through dissemination of research and professional opportunities in local community for undergraduate students interested in the Behavior Analysis field. 

WATCH VIDEO
summary: The UBAC is social and academic club created to promote and support academic interests and professional development of UNR students through research and professional opportunities as well as opportunity to engage with peers.

name: Nevada Hockey Club
summary: Providing hockey for people of skill levels in both ice and roller to have fun and develop.

name: University of Nevada Education Association
description:
The University of Nevada Education Association (U of NEA) is primarily focused on benefiting future educators, but is open to all UNR students. We offer events and benefits for paid and unpaid members, including national events hosted by the National Education Association (NEA), professional development events/speakers, community service engagement, and more. Our current goals include building our member base, expanding our professional development offerings, and working closer with local schools, businesses, or administrations to build mutually beneficial relationships.

If you would like to learn more or are interested in joining, please reach out to us:

Instagram: @uofnea / https://www.instagram.com/uofnea/
2024-25 president: Miles Shanor / mshanor@unr.edu

Email: nevadaeducationassociation@gmail.com
summary: UNR's branch of the National Education Association, the largest labor union in the country. Our club is dedicated to bringing together future educators and offering them opportunities for community engagement, personal growth, and collaboration.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) is reactivating at the University of Nevada, Reno! The goal of this organization is to create a community (or familia) that emphasizes professional and academic support to students who are also passionate about increasing diversity/representation in their respective STEM fields. Professional Development

Conferences

Scholarships

Supportive of Hispanics/Diversity in STEM (does not have to be Hispanic or speak Spanish)

Undergraduate/Graduate students

Follow us!

Twitter: ShpeUnr

Instagram: shpeunr

Slack: unrshpe.slack.com

LinkedIn: SHPE UNR

Email us! unr.shpe@gmail.com
summary: The goal of this organization is to create a community (or familia) that emphasizes professional and academic support to students who are also passionate about increasing diversity/representation in their respective STEM fields.

name: Material Advantage Club
description:
We are a professional, on-campus club that aims to get students interested in materials. It doesn't matter what your major is: materials are in everything! Our chapter is a part of the national organization known as Material Advantage, which helps students connect with professionals in the field, guest speakers, seminars, conferences and social events.  

This student program is supported by preeminent societies in the materials science and engineering profession: the American Ceramic Society (ACerS); Association for Iron & Steel Technology (AIST); ASM International (the Materials Information Society); and the Minerals, Metals and Materials Society (TMS).
summary: We’re a professional on‑campus club that gets students excited about materials—no matter your major. As part of Material Advantage, we connect members with industry professionals, guest speakers, seminars, conferences, and social events.

name: Theta Tau
summary: Theta Tau is professional co-ed engineering fraternity whose purpose is to develop and maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

name: Newman Club
description:
Our Lady of Wisdom Catholic Church & Newman Center Community provides a wholesome environment for study and recreation for the students and faculty of the University of Nevada, Reno. Located between Argenta and Peavine Residential Hall, the Newman Center arranges free student lunches on Wednesdays as well as monthly service projects, social events, and bible studies throughout the week.

Follow @wolfpackcatholic on Instagram to find out more about upcoming events!
summary: Our Lady of Wisdom Catholic Church & Newman Center Community, located between Argenta and Peavine Residential Hall, provides a wholesome environment for study and relaxation by the students and faculty of the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: Urban Dance Club
summary: Urban Dance Club serves to help its members with get familiarized with these styles through weekly workshops. With a different choreographer each week, there will be nearly endless styles to experience.

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness at UNR
description: NAMI at UNR works to serve UNR's campus by advocating for the mental health and support of its student body. We meet twice a month to serve the Reno community, run groups to talk about student issues (such as burnout, emotional coping, how to balance life, etc), and run workshops showcasing how to help your mental health, or have guest speakers talk about lifestyle.
summary: NAMI at UNR is a philanthropic club whose main purpose is to improve the quality of life for persons who are affected by mental illnesses. We will be providing education, support, and advocacy for those interested in learning more about mental illness.

name: Human Development and Family Sciences Club
description: Follow us on Instagram: @unrhdfsclub for more information!
summary: HDFS Club is a place where those majoring, minoring, or are simply interested in Human Development and Family Sciences can come together. We focus on giving back to the community along with connecting our members to outside organizations.

name: Canada Hall
summary: Canada Hall, a branch of Residential Life, Housing, and Food Services, houses upperclassmen and transfer students, including the Transfer LLC.

name: Great Basin Hall
summary: Built in 2018, Great Basin Hall is primarily suite-style with single, double and triple room options and features some of our Living Learning Communities.

name: Manzanita - Juniper Halls
summary: Juniper Hall offers suites with single-resident rooms designed for returning and transfer students. Built in 1896, Manzanita Hall has undergone renovations to accommodate up to 100 residents.

name: Nevada Living Learning Community
summary: The Living Learning Community building is home to nine sub-LLCs.

name: Nye Hall
summary: Nye's eight coed floors house a total of 555 residents. Living in a traditional residence hall is a great way to meet new people and build a strong sense of Wolf Pack Pride.

name: Peavine Hall
summary: Peavine Hall programs focus on the 7 Dimensions of Wellness and provide an opportunity for students to explore how each dimension of wellness impacts them.

name: Sierra Hall
summary: Sierra Hall includes singles and doubles designed primarily for returning and transfer students.

name: UNR Pre-Vet Club
summary: The Pre-Veterinary Club at the University of Nevada, Reno, offers members invaluable opportunities for professional networking, hands-on experience, volunteer work, and skill development—designed to prepare aspiring veterinarians for veterinary school.

name: Reno Cubing Club
summary: We strive to foster an inclusive community that encourages learning, critical thinking, and sportsmanship through the use and study of Rubik's cube and similar puzzles.

name: Alpha Phi Gamma
description:
Alpha Phi Gamma National Sorority Inc. was established on February 1st, 1994 at California State Polytechnic University Pomona. Seven women founded the sorority: Allex Choi, Candy Cunanan, Christine Nguyen, Sandie Rillera, Kolleen Kim, Grace Hsieh and Jennifer Oku. These Sisters wanted to bring together all women of different nationalities through the bonds of friendship and sisterhood.

On May 21, 2011, the determination and devotion to bring Alpha Phi Gamma to the University of Nevada, Reno became a reality by our 17 charters, known as the Charter Charger Girls Class. We received chapter status on July 13, 2013 and became recognized as the Kappa Chapter. On May 2nd, 2021, Kappa Chapter was re-established by 9 re-charters, known as the Iota Poison Ivy Class. 

Since our establishment, we have grown to have 76 sisters in our chapter and counting!

Alpha Phi Gamma is committed to bettering our communities! Our official philanthropy is the Fight Against Violence Towards Women. We are also part of the National Coalition Against Domestic Violence (NCADV).
summary: Alpha Phi Gamma is an Asian-interest, but not Asian-exclusive, sorority whose purpose is to provide service and Asian Awareness to the university and the community. We promote scholarship, academic excellence, personal development, and leadership.

name: Sigma Tau Gamma
summary: A Fraternity of students dedicated to embodying the virtues of Citizenship, Excellence, Learning, Leadership, Brotherhood, and Integrity.

name: Delta Gamma
description:
A social/service sorority that focuses on fostering high ideals of friendship among women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them the best qualities of character.

https://vimeo.com/429305556?fbclid=IwAR0kkz-3OvoB8PEhh5-BUYWHNp80oAS_jpbJtirkNUdR6oDmxmUlwI-ZhcA
summary: A social/service sorority that focuses on fostering high ideals of friendship among women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them the best qualities of character.

name: Pi Beta Phi Fraternity For Women
description: Pi Beta Phi is a collegiate Fraternity for Women in which we seek to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential, and enrich lives through community service.
summary: The mission of Pi Beta Phi Fraternity for Women is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential and enrich lives through community service.

name: Nevada Cycling
description: The Nevada Cycling club is for UNR students who are seeking to find other students who ride or compete in similar disciplines of cycling as them
summary: Yo what up, we are UNR cycling, the ultimate hub for UNR's biking crew. Our mission? Simple. We're all about bringing together the raddest folks on campus who live for two wheels and good times. We are excited to get a solid group of people together.

name: Nevada Neuro Society
description: Our sister club is Nu Rho Ps, the National Honor Society for Neuroscience majors and minors. However, Nevada Neuro Society is for anyone interested in the brain and field of neuroscience, regardless of their major.
summary: Nevada Neuro Society (NNS) is a community health and outreach club focused on educating and helping people in the greater Reno area. NNS welcomes all members from all backgrounds while encouraging learning and exploration in neuroscience.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: SPS is a chapter based society that exists to help students transform themselves into contributing members of the professional community. Traditional coursework develops only one range of skills. Other skills needed to flourish professionally include effective communication and personal interactions, leadership experience, establishing a personal network of contacts, presenting scholarly work in professional meetings and journals, research experiences, and outreach services to the campus and local communities. Through its members, advisers, chapters, and leadership, SPS enables national initiatives and local impacts within the community. SPS supports students, advisers, and departments to improve the overall community.
summary: The Society of Physics Students (SPS) is an organization by which physics and non-physics students alike may come together and engage with one another, as well as with on- and -off campus communities.

name: Young Democrats of University of Nevada Reno
summary: The Young Democrats of UNR exist to engage and inform young people on democratic and progressive issues. We work heavily with local and national organizations to advocate for equity and justice in our community.

name: UNR Bridge Club
summary: If you love strategy and card games then bridge club is for you. Bridge is a card game that develops critical thinking, creativity, will help you get connections for your career and you can get new friends! We also play a lot of other card games!

name: Wolf Pack Miracle Network
description: Wolf Pack Miracle Network at the University of Nevada, Reno strives to engage the student and local communities to promote children’s health and happiness while fostering the leadership and involvement of participants. Our organization raises funds and awareness for Renown Children’s Hospital in Reno, Nevada, through peer-to-peer fundraising, special events, donations from the community, and engagement with corporate and small business partners in the local area. 100% of our efforts go to promoting the mission and function of Renown Children’s Hospital.
summary: Wolf Pack Miracle Network at the University of Nevada, Reno strives to engage the campus community in leadership and service work while raising awareness and crucial funding for Renown Children’s Hospital.

name: Residence Hall Association
summary: Representative body for students who reside on campus during the school year. From the Leadership Councils elected at the beginning of the fall semester, to executive leadership of the Residence Hall Association, RHA aims to foster a sense of community!

name: Alpha Pi Sigma Sorority Incorporated
description: Alpha Pi Sigma Sorority, Incorporated. is a Latina based sorority that is not exclusive. Members of Alpha Pi Sigma strive academically, personally and professionally in a commitment to excellence with a desire to support one another. Alphas are scholars, leaders, contributors and lifetime members of an infinite Alphahood who hold their values close to heart.
summary: Alpha Pi Sigma Sorority Incorporated is a Latina based sorority that is not exclusive. Members of Alpha Pi Sigma strive academically, personally and professionally in a commitment to excellence with a desire to support one another.

name: Lambda Theta Phi
description:
Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is committed to respect and defend the interests and aspirations of every brother to assist in their individual success and to achieve social justice for all.

We have 180+ chapters nationwide.

The mission of Lambda Theta Phi is to empower the Latin male through scholarship, Brotherhood, opportunity and protection of identity and equality.

IDEALS
1. To establish a brotherhood
2. To establish and maintain a high standard of learning
3. To raise the standards of morality and develop character
4. To respect culture and practice chivalry
5. To promote the spirit of man’s dignity and self-identity
summary: We are an on campus Latin fraternity, though not exclusively Latino, which aims to promote Latin pride and culture to the community through leadership, service, and philanthropy.

name: John Mackay Club
description: Creates a community within the Mackay School of Earth Sciences and Engineering and fosters a professional pride in these sciences. This club provides opportunities for networking between students, professors, future potential employers and the community.
summary: Promote within the Mackay School of Earth Sciences and Engineering a relationship between the earth science disciplines and foster a professional pride in these sciences

name: Nevada Esports
description:
Nevada Esports is, at its simplest, THE video games club of UNR! 

The club hosts 7+ esports teams that compete in Mountain West Varsity tournaments, including but not limited to:

 

- League of Legends 
- Valorant 
- Super Smash Bros. 
- Marvel Rivals 
- Overwatch
- Rocket League 
- Team Fortress 2

 


Our club also hosts social events ranging from small gatherings to massive gaming marathons!

 



Small events could be... 

Pub n' sub Social 
League of Legends World Finals watch party 
Trips to the arcade
Jackbox nights
... so much more! 

Large events happen once a semester and are 14-hour gaming marathons. Each event is Bring your own computer. Food, entertainment and vibes are provided! 

Fall: Welcome Week Lan (Around September) 
Spring: Break the Internet (Around Spring Break) 


Besides in person events, the club also has a popular Discord, where students and community members can connect and play games!

Nevada Esports is NOT limited to just the games we compete in (we have a Minecraft server too!), we invite players of all kinds on all different platforms to come and hang out with us! 

Discord
summary: Our organization is run by students for students with the hope of establishing a well known gaming community, as well as fielding some of the best teams the esports industry has seen.

name: Nevada Womens Club Soccer
summary: Nevada Women's Club Soccer is for those who wish to continue playing soccer at a competitive level without playing on a collegiate team. We practice 2-3 times a week, play 6-10 games a season, and often attend at least one tournament.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association
summary:
Mission Statement:
Educating others about the profession of speech language pathology and audiology and advocating for our profession by giving back to the community.

name: Christian Students at UNR
description: We are Christians who wish to align with God’s purpose revealed in His word and to make student’s precious college years count in His eyes through group fellowship, Bible studies, one-on-one meetups, intercollegiate retreats, and church service gatherings.
summary: We are Christians who love and enjoy God. All are welcome to join us as we learn more about God's word and follow his will in this age.

name: College of Business Career Services
summary: The College of Business Career and Corporate Outreach center engages students with career events to support their advancement into the community.

name: Nevada Club Golf
description:
Nevada Club Golf is dedicated to creating and fostering interest in golf at the University of Nevada, Reno and within the surrounding community. We strive to not only introduce people to the game but to also grow as athletes, golfers, and people.

If you are interested in attending our events or becoming a member, please follow our Instagram (@nevadaclubgolf) and join our email list!
summary: Nevada Club Golf is dedicated to creating and fostering interest in golf at the University of Nevada, Reno and within the surrounding community. We strive to not only introduce people to the game but to also improve as athletes, golfers, and people.

name: Nevada Club Baseball
description: This club is a member of the National Club Baseball Association (NCBA-D1). UNR Club Baseball is all about having fun and developing baseball skills at a competitive college level. As a club we have access to the D1 facilities with the school and host practice multiple times a week working on fundamentals, such as fielding, pitching, throwing, and hitting. On the weekends we usually hold an inter-squad scrimmage or exhibition game against other colleges during the Fall. The Fall for us is all about getting into to baseball shape and working towards being able to compete at a high level in the Spring. In the Spring we have a full season of competitive baseball going against schools such as Cal, Sonoma State, UC Davis, Chico State, San Jose State, and Sac State. We host series at Peccole Park.
summary: UNR Club Baseball is all about having fun and developing baseball skills at a competitive college level.

name: Upward Bound
description: Four federally funded grant programs providing free college preparatory services to 246 first-generation, income-qualified students annually at eight target high schools in Washoe and Lyon counties.
summary: Four federally funded grant programs providing free college preparatory services to 246 first-generation, income-qualified students annually at eight target high schools in Washoe and Lyon counties.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
summary: Sigma Phi Epsilon, SigEp, is redefining fraternity on college campuses across the country. We complement a man’s education by delivering a premier student experience in one of the most formative times of his life.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon Pi
description: Sigma Alpha Epsilon Pi is the youngest—and only independent—Jewish sorority in the United States. Focused on being "Jewish First, Greek Second," SAEPi bridges the gap between the Jewish and Greek Letter Organization communities to create a unique environment that Jewish women can call their own. The Sorority works actively to provide a framework for women to cultivate and experience a Jewish identity, while providing them with the social, educational, and professional development opportunities enjoyed by all sorority members.
summary: Sigma Alpha Epsilon Pi is the youngest—and only independent—Jewish sorority in the United States. Focused on being "Jewish First, Greek Second," SAEPi bridges the gap between the Jewish and Greek Letter Organization communities.

name: Nevada Recovery and Prevention
description: Nevada Recovery and Prevention (NRAP) is a lounge for sober students located on the campus of University of Nevada, Reno, in the William Raggio Building, room 1001. This collegiate recovery program holds space for recovery meetings, such as 12 step meetings, art projects, yoga and all other activities that current students are interested in during a particular semester. The lounge has a peer recovery specialist as well as two faculty members that are available to assist with academic advisement, FASFA and recovery support throughout the semester. NRAP also hosts events off campus to support the holistic wellbeing of the student through rock climbing, lake days, hiking, etc.
summary: NRAP (faculty) provides an environment of nurturing support and peer connections for students recovering from substance and behavioral addictions and students choosing a substance-free lifestyle.

name: Black Student Organization
summary: We, the members of the Black Student Organization, a recognized organization by the Associated Students of the University of Nevada, Reno do establish this club for the purpose of uniting students of color and allies at the University of Nevada, Reno.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority Incorporated
description: How Delta Sigma Theta Made History
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is an organization of college educated women committed to the constructive development of its members and to public service with a primary focus on the Black community.

name: Nevada Student Investment Organization
description: Dedicated to helping its members gain more knowledge about investing, the NSIO is a one of a kind and the only club at the University of Nevada, Reno specializing in the interests of the financial markets such as those of stocks, futures, currencies, and much more. Our goals are to provide students at The University of Nevada, Reno with the necessary techniques to navigate financial markets efficiently. The club has weekly outlooks, activities, and lessons to gain a deeper understanding of the complex investing ecosystem. Through NSIO, we hope to increase investment literacy among students on campus. Join our Discord through this link https://discord.gg/DqskuU8B to stay updated with our meetings, events, workshops, and more.
summary: Our goals are to provide students at The University of Nevada, Reno with the necessary techniques to efficiently and effectively navigate financial markets. Club meetings include education on investments, discussion, and various financial competitions.

name: Mackay Muckers Mining
description: Competitive old fashioned mining practices that revive and continue the legacy of Nevada mining history.
summary: Old fashioned mining practices.

name: UNR Improv
description:
1. UNR Improv is an organization dedicated to teaching students the art of improvisation. We believe that improv can help students learn the values of listening, teamwork, confidence, and having fun! Our schedule consists of practices that help train members for shows, and regular shows throughout the year. Active participants foster connection between themselves and other members, all while honing their performance and communication skills in a fun, engaging manner. This organization is for those who want to improve themselves by improv-ing themselves!

 

UNR improv is an organization that teaches its members all about the art of unscripted acting. What might sound as intimidating is actually an amazing experience for both participants and the audience. During weekly practice members learn all about the art form including a variety of games that touch upon story telling, miming, and of course improvisation! At UNR improv members work together, build their confidence, and create lifelong friends. Join UNR improv today, and see how a little improvisation can improve you!
summary: Make lifelong friends and have fun by learning the art of improvised comedy!

name: Wolf Pack Powerlifting
description: The Wolf Pack Powerlifting Club is a diverse community of students who train and compete in the three disciplines of powerlifting: Squat, Bench, Deadlift. Our goal is to provide members of all skill levels a gateway to the world of competitive powerlifting, and we sponsor our top lifters for at least one officially sanctioned meet per semester.

name: UNR Aerospace Club
description: The UNR Aerospace club was founded on the love and passion for aviation and Aerospace. We offer many opportunities including internships, research grants, and certifications. We run 3 major well organized divisions to encompass many aspects of Aerospace. We have programs centered around aviation, rocketry, and communications. The Rocketry program offers a free certification process all the way through Rocketry Level 2. The aviation branch offers workshops on engineering and aviation skills along with a major project of creating a Design Build Fly race rc plane. The communications branch is working on a Ham Radio to communicate with the international space station. We are very welcoming to incoming members of any skill level and would love to have you join us. Stay tuned as we constantly have opportunities and events open to attend.
summary: We are a club of UNR students passionate about Aerospace Engineering! Anyone who is interested in the Aerospace sector is welcome to join! We want to work on useful and fulfilling Aerospace projects that can increase our skills and knowledge of Aerospace

name: Young Democratic Socialists of Reno
description:
We are the Young Democratic Socialists of Reno (YDS), a youth chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) operating at the University of Nevada, Reno. We were founded in the Fall of 2017 in the wake of the events in Charlottesville, by a group of like-minded students and concerned citizens. We have come together to fight against capitalism and other oppressive systems in our community and our country.
Our mission statement is as follows: "The YDSA’s mission is to educate and organize students and young people in the Reno community, and to play a helpful and principled role in the movement for social justice. Within and throughout this struggle, we will articulate and defend the idea that true diversity, tolerance, and equality is impossible under capitalism. We seek social change which extends democracy into all aspects of life ‐ social, political, and economic. This is the struggle for democratic socialism. Our vision of socialism is profoundly democratic and intersectional, and seeks to address inequality in matters of class, race, ethnicity, gender, ability, and orientation."
summary: We are the Young Democratic Socialists of Reno, centralized at the University of Nevada Reno. We focus on community service and socialist education.

name: NevadaCARES

We do this through various awareness campaigns (mainly stickers), education opportunities, bystander intervention trainees, and with the help of our student PACKtivists. You can connect with us on social media, listen to some of our student-driven podcasts, or apply to be a packtivists at our link tree here: nevadacares
summary: NevadaCARES aims to end power-based personal violence by engaging the campus community in awareness, education, programming, and transformative action.

Our vision is that Black students will graduate with an awareness of who they are so that they may participate in an everchanging landscape of reality for Black people in society.

name: Ronald E. McNair Post-Baccalaureate Achievement Program
description: The McNair Scholars Program is a federal TRIO program funded at 151 institutions across the United States which is designed to prepare undergraduate students for doctoral studies through involvement in research and other scholarly activities. McNair participants are either first-generation college students with financial need, or members of a group that is traditionally underrepresented in graduate education. The McNair Scholars Program provides year-round services to prepare participants for acceptance into the quality graduate programs of their choice. Services include faculty mentoring, 10 week summer research institute, research stipend, GRE preparation, travel to conferences and college tours, graduate school application fee waivers, and much more.

name: Division of Extended Studies
description:
One of the biggest benefits of continuing education is that it can improve one’s skills in a current job or help gain new skills in preparation for a career change. There are also huge benefits for employers who promote continuing education in the workplace. If you enjoy learning or are seeking a higher salary, continuing your education can be done at any stage of your career and life.

A major driving force increasing the demand for continuing education is current economic conditions and uncertainty in the global markets. It is during these times that many people seek to improve their skills to stay hired or find other job opportunities.

There are also major benefits for employers who promote continuing education in the workplace. Employees to seek to improve their skills can be an incredible asset for the company. Continuing education in the workplace can be a win-win for both employees and employers. Companies that are truly interested in the welfare of their employees and their professional development often offer full or partial financial aid packages for training.
summary: The Division of Extended Studies provides Noncredit professional and workforce development courses and certificates in a wide array of up-to-the-minute topics to upskill and reskill.

Academic Mentors supplement the residential experience by providing more extensive academic resources and in-depth individual and group academic attention. Academic Mentors collaborate as a team to provide academic support to all on-campus residents, no matter the residence hall they live in.

name: Alianza
description:
Alianza was founded in 2017 at the University of Nevada, Reno as an association of Chicanx, Latinx and Indigenous faculty and staff members who were interested in shaping and advancing diversity initiatives on campus while addressing issues pertinent to diverse communities.

Mission statement

Alianza is an association dedicated to creating a collaborative and generative environment that addresses topics relevant to Chicanx, Latinx, Indigenous, and other underrepresented groups in order to advance educational, research, professional development, and diversity initiatives at the University of Nevada, Reno and in our respective communities. ¡Que viva la causa!

Purpose

Alianza aims to:

  • Provide networking, professional development and mentoring opportunities
  • Assist with efforts to recruit and retain diverse faculty, staff and students
  • Advance research related to Chicanx, Latinx and Indigenous studies
  • Facilitate critical discourse and shape policies that advance diversity initiatives
  • Communicate effectively and cooperate with the appropriate university officials to address concerns and provide solutions to relevant issues
  • Serve in an advisory capacity to the University administration and other entities on campus
  • Educate the university community and the community at large about Chicanx, Latinx, and Indigenous issues and initiatives
summary: Alianza is an association dedicated to addresses topics relevant to Chicanx, Latinx, Indigenous, and other underrepresented groups in order to advance education, research, professional development, and diversity initiatives at University of Reno.

We typically host three to four events each semester, including guest lectures, film screenings, exhibitions, panels, and an annual Art Writing Symposium.

name: Gerontology Academic Program
description:
Gerontology, the study of aging, is a dynamic, interdisciplinary field. There is a significant need for aging-informed individuals globally. Did you know Nevada state has the second-fastest aging population in the country? Our Gerontology Academic Program (GAP) provides up-to-date knowledge on the biological, physiological, communicative, psychological, social, and health aspects of aging. The GAP curriculum encourages critical thinking, and offers opportunities for service learning, research and innovation. Our students learn that no matter their major or career path, they will inevitably work with, or may be asked to support, people who are aging, either personally or professionally.

The Gerontology Academic Program Minor and Certificate are each designated as a "Program of Merit" by the Academy for Gerontology in Higher Education, making UNR one of only 12 universities nationwide to receive this important designation.

The GAP sponsors the Beta Phi Chapter of the Sigma Phi Omega Honor Society in Gerontology and is affiliated with the UNR Associated Students of Gerontology club. The Sanford Center for Aging awards scholarships each year to declared Gerontology students, and co-sponsors with the GAP two major annual campus events: Aging Appreciation Week and Celebration of Scholars in Aging.
summary: The Gerontology Academic Program, Sanford Center for Aging, offers the interdisciplinary Gerontology Minor (18 credits) and Gerontology Certificate (24 credits),

description: There's never been a better time to be an engineer in Nevada. Our state is taking bold steps to reinvent itself as a high-tech hub, and the College of Engineering plays a critical role in educating the students and supporting the research that will make that vision a reality. Globally competitive, locally engaged.

name: Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description:
Globally competitive curriculum

A well-rounded core curriculum combined with opportunities for students to specialize in a subfield of civil engineering gives our graduates both breadth and depth of engineering knowledge. Additionally, a departmental emphasis on creating life-long learners who can communicate about technical, social and ethical issues in engineering design prepares our graduates to work well in interdisciplinary and team environments.

The bachelor's degree  programs in civil and environmental engineering are both accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET, www.abet.org

World-class faculty

Our faculty are dedicated professionals whose active interest in research puts them at the forefront of current technology. Because classes are relatively small, you will get to know and interact with your professors and fellow students. Many of our undergraduates also participate in ongoing research projects.

Industry partnerships

We work with local professionals such as the Nevada Department of Transportation, Associated General Contractors, and other national and international civil engineering employers to provide meaningful summer cooperative training programs for our students and even full-time employment after graduation.

State-of-the-art facilities

Our seismic engineering facilities include more than 30,000 square feet of laboratory, office and auditorium space for students and researchers.  The Earthquake Engineering Laboratory features three biaxial shake tables and one six degree-of-freedom table. Our Large-Scale Structures Laboratory offers a test floor and strong wall for structural engineering experiments that don't require the shake tables. Together, these two labs give us the largest, most-versatile earthquake engineering facility in the United States. 

Additionally, the University houses the Western Regional Superpave Center, a nationally recognized teaching and research lab within the College of Engineering.
summary: The Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering is training Nevada's next generation of problem solvers.

UNR Generation Action, member of Planned Parenthood Generation Action, at The University of Nevada, Reno believes in the fundamental right of every individual to manage their fertility. GenActNV
supports full access to comprehensive reproductive and complementary health care services in settings that preserve and protect the essential privacy and rights of each individual; advocates public
policies that guarantee these rights and ensure access to such services; and supports access to medically accurate educational programs that enhance understanding of human sexuality.

name: History Department
description:
History is the story of human interactions and change over time. It is diverse and dynamic. Every significant act we do today will amount to some legacy for future generations. As an academic field, history is the study of such acts and a sustained examination of the lives of the individuals who partake in them. Historians can have many backgrounds, ranging from economic historians to those who study the historical contexts of environmental change. Historians may also specialize in social, political, intellectual, diplomatic and cultural experiences.

The Department of History offers several different degrees for students who desire to increase their knowledge on specific historical time-periods and who wish to advance their analytical skills. Students have plenty of opportunities to work closely with our award-winning faculty members who specialize in virtually every time-period and region of the world. View the example here of Assistant Professor Sarah Keyes with her mini-lesson on Margaret Foley (1875–1957), an Irish American based in Boston who served as a labor organizer, social worker and suffragist.
summary: The Department of History offers several different degrees for students who desire to increase their knowledge on specific historical time-periods and who wish to advance their analytical skills.

name: Mental Health First Aid - Brought to you by Counseling Services!
description:
Join Counseling Services and become a certified Mental Health First Aider and help to create a more supportive and mental-health-positive Wolf Pack!

 

Designed by mental health professionals and individuals with lived experience, this engaging training teaches practical skills to help you identify when someone is showing signs of distress, teaches you how to offer support, and guides you on how to connect them to appropriate resources.

 

Mental Health First Aid (MHFA) is a blended training, consisting of a two-hour virtual session followed by a six-hour in person training. Upon completion of the training participants are certified in MHFA for three-years.
summary:

name: Graduate Program of Hydrologic Sciences
description:
The University of Nevada, Reno's Interdisciplinary Hydrologic Science program focuses on studies of water in the environment including its role in geologic and biogeochemical processes, ecosystem functions, and climate science.

Our hydrologic sciences research faculty have expertise in contaminant transport, watershed hydrology, ecohydrology, aqueous geochemistry, global climate change, groundwater hydraulics, vadose zone hydrology, surface water hydrology and water resources engineering. Our faculty come together from more than eight units across campus and the Reno area. 

 

The goals of the program are to provide fundamental and advanced training to students in the critical fields of surface and subsurface hydrology. Over 70 faculty at the University of Nevada, Reno, DRI, and agencies such as the U.S. Forest Service and the Agricultural Research Service conduct research in areas such as contaminant transport (surface and subsurface), watershed hydrology, ecohydrology, aqueous geochemistry, global climate change, groundwater hydraulics, vadose zone hydrology, surface water hydrology and water resources engineering. Thus, students have a tremendous range of options to study.

In addition to the outstanding educational opportunities, Reno and its surroundings offer tremendous opportunities for recreation. The Reno-Tahoe area offers some of the greatest skiing, mountain biking, climbing and kayaking of any university. Whether it's kayaking at the new Reno Whitewater Park (10 minutes from campus) or sledding at Squaw Valley (40 minutes from campus), students can unwind from their studies.
summary: The University of Nevada, Reno's Interdisciplinary Hydrologic Science program focuses on studies of water in the environment including its role in geologic and biogeochemical processes, ecosystem functions, and climate science.

We work collaboratively with faculty and staff to provide the best-possible animal care, optimizing animal welfare per the needs of each species and thereby also helping to yield high quality science. We do campus-wide veterinary care, including for the livestock populations located on properties of the Nevada Experimental Agriculture Station (NAES). Our staff provides daily husbandry and research services within the centralized and de-centralized animal facilities.

The office reports to Research & Innovation and the Vice President for Research and Innovation (the Institutional Official) throughout all of the University's programs of animal care and use. Animal Resources is a component of the University's PHS Animal Welfare Assurance and is a USDA registered research facility. Our mission is well aligned with the University community at large and holds the same values of excellence, integrity, inclusiveness and collaboration.
summary: As a service division with UNR's Research & Innovation, the Office of animal Resources provides optimized animal care and welfare for high quality science, campus-wide veterinary care, and assistance with faculty research and teaching programs.

The Muslim Students Association (MSA) is a religious, social, and outreach student organization that aims to bring together the Muslim students on campus, and in the greater Northern Nevada area, as well as serve their voice in the community. We provide a safe place for individuals with multiple points of entry to learn, pray, socialize and utilize a space situated at intellectual and spiritual crossroads. The goals of the club can be briefed as:

  1. To create an environment on campus that will foster the development and growth of the diverse Muslim community at the University of Nevada, Reno.

  2. To provide Muslims with proper accommodations to fulfill religious obligations.

  3. To sponsor programming and events that positively contribute to the development of Islam and Muslims on campus. This may include lectures, panel discussions, halaqas (Islamic discussion groups) as well as social events.

  4. To act as a resource to the whole campus community and represent Islam in the best possible manner.

  5. To actively engage both Muslims and non-Muslims in a portrayal of the message of Islam as completely and accurately as possible.

name: Social Psychology Program
description:
Our interdisciplinary doctoral program is founded on a vision of social psychology as the core discipline of human affairs. It represents an integration of psychological and sociological scholarship with a bio-psycho-social foundation, grounded in culture and gender, to form an interdisciplinary, contextualized perspective for the investigation of personal and social life.

Our scholarship integrates the study of individuals, the small groups in which they interact, and the context of larger systems and organizations within which they exist.

The mission of our doctoral program is to advance scientific knowledge of social psychological structures and processes, and to do so through scholarly study, training of Ph.D. students, and dissemination of our knowledge in both scientific and applied communities.

We are deeply committed to the guidance and instruction of graduate students as both junior colleagues and advanced students.
summary: The Interdisciplinary Social Psychology Ph.D. program applies rigorous psychological and sociological scholarship to better understand the processes, structures and contexts that impact social interactions.

description:
Using the expertise and resources of University of Nevada, Reno and the nationwide land-grant university system, Extension’s 4-H Youth Development Program provides youth (ages 5-19) with hands-on, peer-driven learning experiences in agricultural science, STEM, civic engagement, healthy living and outdoor education through club, camp, in-school and after-school programs.

Young people who participate in Nevada 4-H learn essential skills such as leadership, problem solving, conflict resolution, public speaking and communication that are needed to succeed in school, in the workplace and in life.

4-H programs are grounded in the belief that kids learn best by doing. Kids and teens complete hands-on projects in a positive environment where they receive guidance from adult mentors and are encouraged to take on proactive leadership roles. Kids can concentrate on one focus area ortry a variety of programs throughout their 4-H experience.
summary: Using the expertise and resources of University of Nevada, Reno and the nationwide land-grant university system, Extension’s 4-H Youth Development Program provides youth (ages 5-19) with hands-on, peer-driven learning experiences across the state.

name: The University of Nevada, Reno Student Chapter of The Wildlife Society
description:
The UNR student chapter of The Wildlife Society is dedicated to providing both professional and recreational opportunities for students interested in wildlife ecology. We meet once a month via Zoom and hold in-person gatherings throughout the year that aim to bring students from all majors closer to wildlife and the natural world.

Founded in the fall of 2007, UNR's student-run chapter is one of many parts of the National Wildlife Society. We encourage all students interested in field biology, wildlife, or anything related to getting outside to join. Professional opportunities are endless for those within the field. Our club runs hikes, professional development workshops, grad student talks, and community volunteering, all designed to foster environmental stewardship and prepare students to thrive in this competitive field at a national level.

Our student chapter welcomes all majors, and membership is open year-round. To join, please attend one of our meetings posted on PackLife and our Instagram (@unrtws)! If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to email student chapter president Sarah Lange, unevadatws@gmail.com, for more information! We are happy to answer any questions you might have :)
summary: Join a club dedicated to connecting students in wildlife and other environmental fields through hands-on experiences and professional networking. While we focus on wildlife, all are welcome to explore and connect with us!

name: Chabad at UNR
description:
Chabad at UNR is dedicated to providing every student regardless of background, philosophy or level of commitment, an open door environment for strengthening and enhancing Jewish life on campus. Chabad serves individuals looking for an anchor and non-judgmental, accepting, personalized Jewish experience.

Together we strive to foster a familiar environment, emphasizing tradition and Jewish unity. Our goal is to create a place where every Jewish student, from any background and affiliation, feels welcome and accepted. Inspiring Jewish students to find themselves as Jews and progress continuously on a spiritual path, passing on Jewish traditions to family and friends. We focus on the individual as well as the collective and we promote active involvement in all things Jewish, as we encourage young Jews to form intra-religious relationships and enhance their Jewish identities.

The college years are a critical stage in development, when students exercise their independence and make many of the decisions that will help shape their lives. As a home away from home the Center offer students of all affiliations and backgrounds an opportunity to enhance their college years and experience the richness of their Jewish heritage. By participating in religious and social events, students meet other Jewish friends in a warm and inviting atmosphere and create friendships that will last a lifetime.

Stop by the Center any time for a discussion, a cup of coffee, a smile or a hello. Your Family is here to help you, whatever the need may be, that’s what we’re here for!

We look forward to seeing you soon, Go Pack!
summary: Our mission is a simple one, to become a home for every Jew at UNR. No matter the background, or religious affiliation, we are here to provide a warm and comfortable environment for all.

NevadaFIT

NevadaFIT is a required, one-credit academic program that kicks off the fall semester for first-year students. After completing NevadaFIT, students are better prepared for their first semester of college.

All sessions are conducted by second and third-year graduate students in the practicum or internship phase of the program. Supervision is an important training tool for our program and all sessions at the clinic are recorded to assist in the teaching process. name: University of Nevada, Reno Debate Team
description:
The Nevada Debate team competes in various speech and debate events at colleges and universities both regionally and nationally throughout both the Fall and Spring semesters. The 11 individual events offered by AFA-NIET and NFA are similar to events offered in high school competition. Collegiate debate formats vary widely. In the past, Nevada Debate has competed in Policy, Parli, Lincoln Douglas, British Parliamentary Debate, and International Public Debate. 

With a history dating back to the early 1900's, the forensics program (speech and debate) has a rich tradition at Nevada. Over the years the program has produced numerous accomplished alumni. More recently, the program has won 10 National Titles in the last 15 years across various formats. 

The team is based in the Department of Communication Studies within the College of Liberal Arts at the University of Nevada, Reno. Students on the debate team take classes in Communication Studies (Com 209, 309, 409) and can minor in Debate. Scholarships are available to students who qualify.
summary: The Nevada Debate Team is an intercollegiate competitive speech and debate program in the college of Liberal Arts at the University of Nevada, Reno.

Our mission is to provide opportunities for students to grow as performers, teachers, and scholars through workshops, masterclasses, recitals, and community engagement.

As part of a nationwide network, our chapter connects members with resources and mentorship from distinguished professionals in the field of music education, offering valuable preparation for future careers in teaching and performance. We encourage collaboration among students and faculty, promote innovative teaching practices, and advocate for the role of music in enriching lives and communities.

Importantly, our chapter contributes to the vibrant local music scene through its involvement with the Northern Nevada MTA, providing opportunities for outreach, service, and performance within the Reno–Tahoe community. By engaging with local educators, students, and audiences, we help strengthen music education in Northern Nevada while preparing our members to become leaders in the profession.

By participating in our organization, members contribute to a supportive professional community, gain leadership experience, and play an active role in strengthening the presence of music education at UNR and beyond.

name: Pack Provisions
description:
Pack Provisions is the on-campus food pantry that serves the University of Nevada, Reno community. This is a completely free resource that is available for all members of the Wolf Pack to utilize whenever they are in need. This resource can be accessed by students, staff, and faculty. Our pantry is located inside of the Center for Student Engagement, which is on the third floor of the Joe Crowley Student Union.

 

Pack Provisions strives to support all members of the University of Nevada, Reno to achieve academic success by providing access to basic necessities such as food and hygiene supplies. This mission is carried out by eliminating barriers that University community members may have while experiencing financial hardship. Pack Provisions provides social wellness through perishable and non-perishable foods, meal swipes, grocery funds, school supplies, hygiene items and more.

 

This service is not government funded; therefore, it is not considered a public charge benefit and will not affect the status of international students. This service does not require a social security number, state ID, or a passport to request services. This service does not impact financial aid awards. The only requirement for utilizing Pack Provisions is an active NSHE ID number.

 

If you have any questions about your eligibility to use these services, please email packprovisions@asun.unr.edu.
summary: Pack Provisions is the on-campus food pantry that serves the University of Nevada, Reno community. This is a completely free resource that is available for all members of the Wolf Pack to utilize whenever they are in need.

name: University of Nevada Reno Rodeo Team
description:
The Team

 

The University of Nevada, Reno College Rodeo Team, established in 1971, is a proud member of the National Intercollegiate Rodeo Association. We aim to promote and develop rodeo at the college level, ensuring that students have access to educational and competitive opportunities. Our program is built on the principles of sportsmanship and conduct, striving to uphold the highest standards in these areas. We are committed to preserving the rich heritage of the western lifestyle and maintaining an unwavering regard for animal welfare. Through our program, we strive to foster a strong community that supports the growth and success of our student-athletes both in and out of the arena.

 

The Facility

 

Our program boasts a comprehensive 12-acre facility designed to support a variety of rodeo activities and events. This includes two barns, three arenas—one of which is an indoor arena—over 40 covered stalls, and 15 uncovered stalls. Additionally, our facility features a round pen, multiple turnouts, and extensive trailer parking to accommodate our team's needs.

 

The Coaches

 

Leadership and coaching are at the heart of our program. Our coaching staff is led by the accomplished Lita Scott, a three-time National Finals Rodeo barrel racing qualifier with a rich background in reined cow horse events as well. Lita's achievements also include being a 20-time California Circuit finalist, which underscores her expertise and dedication to the sport. Our assistant coach, Denton Mars, is an alumnus of Northwestern Oklahoma State University, where he competed in team roping, saddle bronc riding, and calf roping. Denton is a California Circuit Finals saddle bronc rider and is currently one of the top-ranked bronc riders in California. His extensive experience and success in various rodeo events make him an invaluable asset to our team.
summary: The mission of the University of Nevada Reno Rodeo Team (UNRRT) is to promote and develop rodeo at the college level and provide educational and competitive opportunities for students.

name: Nevada Airsoft Club
description:
The official home of airsoft at UNR! We attend free weekly events in the Reno-Tahoe area in association with local airsoft groups like The Brushwolves, Wolves of War, Sierra Nevada Rangers, and The Reno Regulators.

Make sure to join the discord for carpool and loaner gear and interact with other members!

Member benefits:

FREE weekly events in the Reno/Tahoe area!

FREE loaner replicas and equipment!

FREE repair and upgrade labor from our in-house tech!

FAQ's:

Q: What is Airsoft?

A: TLDR, It's a FPS game brought to life! Similar to NERF or paintball, we use replica weapons that shoot 6mm plastic bb's. These hit much harder and are more accurate than NERF darts, but don't have the raw bruising power that paintball tends to have (and you don't get paint all over your gear!).

Q: Where do we play?

A: Most groups in the Reno area play out in BLM land (Bureau of Land Management). The community as a whole works to switch up the fields on a week-to-week basis, with the places we play scattered across desert, sageland and forest areas around Reno. The weekly free games are kept within a 45-60min drive from campus, however some larger paid events at different fields are attended by some members down around the Sacramento area.

Q: What do I need to start playing?

A: Not much! If it's your first time trying out the sport, we recommend a couple layers of clothes so you can get used to what it feels like when you get hit. It doesn't hurt a lot, but it can leave a welt if you are hit up close on bare skin. Hoodies or flannels are pretty solid options, but even something like a long-sleeve shirt can help. In addition, we also highly recommend a broken in set of boots or closed-toed footwear and enough water and snacks for a day out in the field.

Q: What if I don't have a replica?

A: We have a stock of loaner replicas and gear maintained by the club that are free to use by all members. These are first come, first served, but you can make sure you're geared up by requisitioning them here! (in our discord)

Q: What if I don't have any gear?

A: Don't sweat it! We require that all participants wear ANSI-rated eye protection during events, but If you don't have any, we will always have spares on hand. We also have a stock of spare tactical vests but like our stock of replicas, these are first-come first-served. 

Q: What if I don't have a ride?

A: We carpool! Feel free to request a seat in this channel on our discord!
summary: The official UNR Airsoft Club is back in force for spring 2025! Attending FREE weekly events, all ya need to do is bring that wolf pack spirit and get ready to sling some plastic!

name: Pack Mentor Program
description:
The Pack Mentor Program pairs ﬁrst-year students with experienced upperclassmen who are committed to making your adjustment to The Wolf Pack as seamless as possible. Your mentor is not just a guide; they are your personal resource who can provide social and academic support as well as ongoing advice about how to succeed at UNR.

 

How the Program Works

 

Personalized Mentorship: You will be paired with a student leader, your mentor, who will be there to assist you on a one-on-one basis.

 

Tailored Support: Your mentor will work closely with you to identify your specific needs and concerns.

 

Student-Centric Approach: The Pack Mentorship Program is by students, for students. We believe in creating a supportive community where everyone feels valued and understood.

 

Easy Transition: The Pack Mentorship Program is tailored to cater to your unique needs, ensuring that your transition into college is not a maze but a well-guided path.

 

Benefits of Becoming a Mentee

 

Making the transition to college life can be challenging. Many first-year students struggle to meet others, manage stress and other emotions, meet academic demands, and find a sense of belonging to campus. Peer Mentors are here to make your transition to UNR by providing support and connection. Being a mentee allows you to:

  • Gain advice, encouragement and support from upperclassmen
  • Learn from the experiences of others
  • Develop strategies for dealing with academic and personal issues
  • Identify academic and personal goals
  • Build strong relationships with other students
  • Learn the Wolf Pack Way

 

For more information click on the Contact Button.

Vision: The Wolf Pack Battalion cultivates character-driven leaders capable of effective leadership in various contexts while nurturing responsible citizens dedicated to serving the United States.

Purpose:

  1. Leadership: Developing confident, ethical leaders.
  2. Academics: Promoting well-rounded academic excellence.
  3. Character: Instilling integrity and discipline.
  4. Fitness: Ensuring physical readiness for leadership.
  5. Community: Fostering civic-minded leaders.
  6. Military Readiness: Preparing for commissioned officer roles.
  7. Adaptability: Equipping for dynamic environments.

 

History: The University of Nevada (UNR) ROTC Wolf Pack Battalion traces its origins back to 1888 when it became part of the "University Corps of Cadets." The ROTC officially began with the passage of the National Defense Act on June 3, 1916. It provided the opportunity for commissions in the Army as Second Lieutenants through a "Four Year Program." The formation of the "University Corps of Cadets" was mandated by the Morrill Act of 1862 and the Nevada Constitution of 1864, which required the availability of military science and tactics courses at the university. Classes commenced on the Reno campus in 1896, coinciding with the opening of Morrill Hall. As the 20th century approached and during World War I, there was an anticipation that all eligible men under 27 would enroll as Cadets for their entire four undergraduate years. However, from 1888 to 1916, only the top three candidates received recommendations for commissioning. It wasn't until after World War I that students who completed the mandatory four years of training became eligible for commissions. After the war, general enrollment at UNR surged to 500 students and expanded to 1,000 students by 1936.

 

World War II marked the most extensive military mobilization in U.S. history. In 1943, as part of this nationwide effort, the Army and the Army Air Force established a training presence at the University of Nevada, Reno, to educate officers and aircrews. Lincoln Hall was the primary administrative center during this period, which continued until the war's conclusion in 1945. Following World War II and throughout the 1960s, the Wolf Pack Battalion saw a decline in ROTC participation. Nevertheless, enacting the ROTC Vitalization Act of 1964 revitalized enrollment by introducing an innovative option—the "two-year program." This program enabled students to complete a condensed six-week summer introductory course, positioning them to begin the ROTC advanced program in their junior year. This transformation injected fresh vitality into the Wolf Pack Battalion.

 

Present Day: Today, the Battalion's lasting legacy persists in shaping the leaders of tomorrow. The Wolf Pack Battalion proudly hosts a thriving community of Cadets and cadre. Our alums actively participate in missions and contingency operations across the globe, with dedicated service in regions such as Afghanistan, Iraq, and Kuwait. Astonishingly, nearly a century since its inception, the national ROTC program produces 65 percent of U.S. Army Officers.
summary: UNR's ROTC shapes ethical leaders through rigorous training since 1888. Focused on leadership, academics, character, fitness, community, military readiness, and adaptability, it has 120+ active Cadets, contributing to its enduring Legacy.

name: Payment 4 Placements - UNR
description:
1. We believe that all social work students should be paid for their internships.
  2. We believe that paid internships will free students from financial hardship.
  3. We believe that paid internships will make social work education more inclusive and accessible, reducing the barriers to obtaining social work employment. This, in turn, will make the social work profession more socioeconomically and racially diverse. 
  4. We believe that securing paid internships for all social work students across the U.S. is tied to struggles for racial, economic, and gender justice, particularly those led by labor movements. As such, we strive to act in solidarity with all oppressed workers fighting for dignity and better working conditions. 
  5. We believe that the contributions of social work students are essential to our internship sites. Many student interns do the exact same or similar tasks as those performed by their sites’ paid staff; some internship sites could not function without them.
  6. We believe that paying all social work students for their internships will be an historic investment in care work, which is a type of feminized and racialized work that employers often undervalue.
  7. We recognize that underinvestment in care work affects many of our internship sites, too. So we will secure payment for all internships through a combination of university, local, state, and federal funding, and through policy change within legislatures and the CSWE.
  8. We celebrate that every student body’s path to paid internships will be unique.
  9. We act in solidarity with students at all degree levels and in other disciplines. We encourage them to join us in demanding paid internships.
  10. We believe that winning paid internships is an important step toward solving the problems endemic to the profession of social work. It is the beginning, not the end, of holding the social work profession accountable to its stated values of racial, economic, and gender justice.
summary: P4P is a nationwide movement calling for all social work students to be paid for their degree-required internships.
